item.title: DMARC 如何使用子域（DMARC sp 标签）？ | How DMARC Works With Subdomains (DMARC sp Tag)?
item.description:
我们将在本文中讨论一个较少讨论的 DMARC 主题：DMARC 如何在子域场景中工作？

具体来说，我们将在下面解决这些问题：

  • 应该使用 sp 标签来保护子域吗？

在深入了解各种 DMARC 子域场景之前，我们需要介绍组织域的概念。

组织域是域名的“根域/顶级域”。 即，删除了所有子域的域名。 以下是一些域名及其组织域：

域名组织领域
例子.com 例子.com
例子.com
例子.com
例子.co.us

组织域是通过拆分主题域名来确定的，然后将这些部分与包含顶级域 (TLD) 的公共后缀列表 (PSL) 匹配，包括国家 TLD，例如美国的“.co.us”和“ .co.uk”在英国

这是由 Mozilla 基金会维护的PSL 。

当电子邮件到达接收服务器时，它需要对电子邮件应用 DMARC。 为此，服务器尝试通过称为 DMARC 策略发现的过程找到 DMARC 策略。

确定电子邮件消息的RFC5322.From域；

  • 在 DNS 中查询RFC5322 上的 DMARC 记录。从步骤 1 中找到的域； 根据结果​​：
    • 如果只找到 1 条 DMARC 记录，则返回记录中的策略；
    • 如果找到多个 DMARC 记录，则策略发现终止并且 DMARC 处理不应用于此消息；
    • 如果没有找到 DMARC 记录，则执行步骤 3；
  • 在 RFC5322 的组织域上查询 DNS 的 DMARC 记录。如果 2 个域不同，则来自域； 根据结果​​：
    • 如果只找到 1 条 DMARC 记录，则返回记录中的策略；
    • 如果没有找到 DMARC 记录或找到多个 DMARC 记录，则策略发现终止并且 DMARC 处理不应用于此消息；
  • 如果检索到的策略不包含有效的 p 标签：
    • 如果存在具有至少一个有效报告 URI 的 rua 标记，则接收服务器将 p=none 应用于消息；
    • 否则，策略发现终止并且 DMARC 处理不适用于此消息；

    • 请注意，在每个 DMARC 策略发现过程中最多发出 2 个 DNS 查询。 一个用于 RFC5322.From 域，另一个用于组织域（如果与 RFC5322.From 域不同）。

    如果您有多个级别的子域，则不会为中间子域发出 DNS 查询。 由于子域上的多个 DNS 查询，这对于减少 DNS 的工作量是必要的。

    例如，如果 RFC5322.From 域是 it.sales.example.com，则只会为 it.sales.example.com 发出 1 个 DNS 查询，并且可能为 example.com 发出 1 个 DNS 查询。 不会为 sales.example.com 发出 DNS 查询，因为它是一个中间子域。

    场景：子域继承组织域的p策略

    如果组织域有一个带有策略（p 标签）但没有子域策略（sp 标签）的 DMARC 记录，而子域没有 DMARC 记录，则子域继承组织域的 p 策略。

    例如，如果在 example.com 上发布了 DMARC 记录，如下所示：

     example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject;"

    现在 example.com 的策略是拒绝，任何没有 DMARC 记录的 example.com 的子域都将具有拒绝策略。

    场景：子域继承组织域的sp策略

    如果组织域具有带有策略（p 标签）和子域策略（sp 标签）的 DMARC 记录，而子域没有 DMARC 记录，则子域继承组织域的子域策略。

    例如，如果在 example.com 上发布了 DMARC 记录，如下所示：

     example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=quarantine;"

    由于 example.com 的 sp 策略明确设置为隔离，因此任何 example.com 的没有 DMARC 记录的子域都将具有隔离策略。

    场景：子域覆盖组织域的策略

    如果组织域具有带有策略（p 标记）和子域策略（sp 标记）的 DMARC 记录，而子域具有自己的策略（p 标记），则子域会用自己的策略覆盖组织域的子域策略。

    例如，如果在 example.com 上发布了 DMARC 记录，如下所示：

     example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=reject;"

    以及在 sales.example.com 上发布的 DMARC 记录：

     sales.example.com "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine;"

    在这种情况下，sales.example.com 自己的策略会覆盖 example.com 的策略。 因此，p=quarantine 应用于 sales.example.com。

    场景：在子域上发布子域策略

    当在子域上发布的 DMARC 记录中使用 sp 标签时，由于 DMARC 策略发现过程的影响，sp 标签将被忽略。

    例如，如果您在子域上有 DMARC 记录：

     sales.example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=quarantine;"

    带有子域的 DMARC 最佳实践

    在您的组织域上达到 p=reject 后，您还应该使用 p=reject 保护您的子域。 这是因为即使您的子域的 DMARC 策略是 p=none 或 p=quarantine，攻击者仍然可以代表您的子域发送电子邮件。

    这可以通过将组织域的策略设置为 p=reject 轻松实现，并且不要在任何子域上覆盖它。 这样，您组织域下的所有子域都将具有 p=reject，并且没有明确授权的任何人都不能代表您的组织发送电子邮件！

    相关文章：

    原始帖子： DMARC 如何与子域一起使用（DMARC sp 标签）？



    原文:


    We will cover a lesser discussed DMARC topic in this article: how does DMARC work in subdomain scenarios?

    Specifically, we will address these questions below:

    • how DMARC policy discovery works?
    • how DMARC policy inheritance works?
    • should one use the sp tag to protect subdomains?

    Before diving into various DMARC subdomain scenarios, we need to introduce the concept of organizational domain.

    What is an organizational domain in DMARC?

    An organizational domain is the "root domain/top-level domain" of a domain name. That is, a domain name with all the subdomains removed. The following is a few domain names and their organizational domains:

    Domain Name Organizational Domain
    example.com example.com
    sales.example.com example.com
    mail.sales.example.com example.com
    sales.example.co.us example.co.us

    The organizational domain is determined by breaking the subject domain name, then matching the parts with a public suffix list (PSL) containing top-level domains (TLDs), including country TLDs, e.g., ".co.us" in the U.S. and ".co.uk" in the U.K.

    Here is a PSL maintained by Mozilla Foundation.

    How DMARC policy discovery works

    When an email message arrives at the receiving server, it needs to apply DMARC to the email message. In order to do this, the server tries to find the DMARC policy via a process known as DMARC policy discovery.

    DMARC policy discovery goes through these steps to find the DMARC policy for an incoming email message:

    1. Determine the RFC5322.From domain of the email message;
    2. Query the DNS for a DMARC record on the RFC5322.From domain found in step 1; depending on the outcome:
      • if only 1 DMARC record is found, the policy in the record is returned;
      • if multiple DMARC records are found, policy discovery terminates and DMARC processing is not applied to this message;
      • if no DMARC record is found, go to step 3;
    3. Query the DNS for a DMARC record on the organizational domain of the RFC5322.From domain, if the 2 domains are different; depending on the outcome:
      • if only 1 DMARC record is found, the policy in the record is returned;
      • if no DMARC record is found or multiple DMARC records are found, policy discovery terminates and DMARC processing is not applied to this message;
    4. If a retrieved policy doesn't contain a valid p tag:
      • if a rua tag with at least one valid reporting URI is present, the receiving server applies p=none to the message;
      • otherwise, policy discovery terminates and DMARC processing is not applied to this message;

    Note that at most 2 DNS queries are issued in each DMARC policy discovery process. One for the RFC5322.From domain, and the other for the organizational domain (if different than the RFC5322.From domain).

    If you have multiple levels of subdomains, no DNS query will be issued for the intermediate subdomains. This is necessary to reduce the workload on the DNS due to multiple DNS queries on subdomains.

    For example, if the RFC5322.From domain is it.sales.example.com, only 1 DNS query will be issued for it.sales.example.com, and possibly 1 DNS query for example.com. No DNS query will be issued for sales.example.com, as it's an intermediate subdomain.

    How DMARC policy inheritance works

    The DMARC policy inheritance mechanism allows domain owners to set DMARC policies on subdomains in a flexible way.

    The DMARC policy inheritance rules work like below.

    Scenario: subdomain inherits organizational domain's p policy

    If the organizational domain has a DMARC record with a policy (p tag) but no subdomain policy (sp tag), while the subdomain doesn't have a DMARC record, the subdomain inherits the organizational domain's p policy.

    For example, if there is a DMARC record published on example.com, as shown below:

    example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject;"

    Now that example.com's policy is reject, any example.com's subdomain without a DMARC record will have a reject policy.

    Scenario: subdomain inherits organizational domain's sp policy

    If the organizational domain has a DMARC record with a policy (p tag) and a subdomain policy (sp tag), while the subdomain doesn't have a DMARC record, the subdomain inherits the organizational domain's subdomain policy.

    For example, if there is a DMARC record published on example.com, as shown below:

    example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=quarantine;"

    Since example.com's sp policy is explicitly set to quarantine, any example.com's subdomain without a DMARC record will have a quarantine policy.

    Scenario: subdomain overrides organizational domain's policy

    If the organizational domain has a DMARC record with a policy (p tag) and a subdomain policy (sp tag), while the subdomain has its own policy (p tag), the subdomain overrides the organizational domain's subdomain policy with its own policy.

    For example, if there is a DMARC record published on example.com, as shown below:

    example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=reject;"

    and a DMARC record published on sales.example.com:

    sales.example.com "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine;"

    In this scenario, sales.example.com's own policy overrides example.com's policy. Therefore, p=quarantine is applied to sales.example.com.

    Scenario: subdomain policy published on subdomain

    When an sp tag is used in a DMARC record published on a subdomain, the sp tag will be ignored due to the effect of the DMARC policy discovery process.

    For example, if you have a DMARC record on a subdomain:

    sales.example.com "v=DMARC1; p=reject; sp=quarantine;"

    the sp tag has no effect on sales.example.com or any subdomains under sales.example.com such as it.sales.example.com.

    Best practices for DMARC with subdomains

    After you reach p=reject on your organizational domain, you should also protect your subdomains with p=reject. This is because even if your subdomains' DMARC policy is p=none or p=quarantine, adversaries can still deliver emails on behalf of your subdomains.

    This can be easily achieved by setting your organizational domain's policy to p=reject, and don't override it on any subdomains. This way, all the subdomains under your organizational domain will have p=reject, and no one without explicit authorization can deliver emails on behalf of your organization!

    Related posts:

    Original post: How DMARC Works With Subdomains (DMARC sp Tag)?
item.author: getdmarcly

item.title: 使用 Next.js 和 PlanetScale 提供服务 | Made service with Next.js and PlanetScale
item.description:
我使用 Flutter 的联系表单小部件创建了“Contact Nite”联系管理服务。

如上图，您可以通过简单的代码粘贴来放置联系表单小部件。

您可以在此服务中查看联系人。

服务
酒馆
提供免费计划。 如果您有兴趣，请尝试。 我也在我的应用程序上使用。

如果有联系，请通过电子邮件和 Slack 通知进行通知。 您可以在该服务上发送和接收邮件。 您无需打开 Gmail。

技术

服务由 Next.js、PlanetScale、Cloud Run 提供。

行星秤

我在开发的时候就知道了。

最近，我在制作小型 Web 应用程序时使用了 Firestore。 因为没有廉价和大容量的服务。 但是当我找到 PlanetScale 时，我试图将 DB 更改为它。 因为它有免费计划和大容量。 我喜欢 MySQL 而不是 NoSQL，所以我很兴奋。

我想我在制作东西时总是使用 PlanetScale。

由 Next.js 制作。 如此简单。 没有什么可考虑的客户端和服务器。 就做吧。 如果我在工作，使用 API Routes 是不好的。 但是在我的独立黑客中，我不能使用其他人。

使用国际化路由进行英语和日语本地化。

部署

使用云运行。 最近我只用这个。 所以我现在很容易用它来开发。

默认情况下，Cloud Run 在代码推送时进行部署。 但是测试比 GitHub Actions 更容易，如下所示：push -> Test on GitHub Actions -> Build on Cloud Build (Starts with Webhook from Actions) -> Deploy to Cloud Run。

数据库迁移也在 Cloud Build 上执行。

在测试中，使用 Jest 的简单 API 测试和 Cypress 的 E2E 测试。 赛普拉斯非常有趣。 在 Cypress Dashboard 上，您可以看到测试视频。

邮件

使用发送网格。 使用动态模板。 您不需要在您的服务上管理邮件模板。

并通过接收挂钩，将收到的邮件保存到数据库。 因此，您只能在网络上发送和接收邮件。

扑

本土化

本地化很复杂。 开发包时，使用使用包创建的示例项目。 在上面开发时，没有任何问题。 但是当我在其他应用程序中使用该包时，本地化效果不佳。 修复很困难。

冷冻

没问题。 我可以用。

测试

包是公开的。 我可以无限制地使用 GitHub Actions。 所以我写了一些测试。

小故事

Contact form on 'Contact Nite' is using its contact form API. “Contact Nite”上的联系表格正在使用其联系表格 API。



原文:

I made a service 'Contact Nite' contact management service with contact form widget for Flutter.

Like above image, you can place contact form widget by just a easy code pasting.

And you can see contacts in this service.

Service
https://contact-nite.com/ja

Pub
https://pub.dev/packages/contact_form

Free plan is provided. If you are interested in, try. I'm also using on my app.

If contacts came, notify by email and Slack notification. And you can send and receive mail on the service. You don't need to open Gmail.

Technology

Package is a simple Flutter package. Service is made by Next.js, PlanetScale, Cloud Run.

PlanetScale

PlanetScale is a Serverless MySQL Service. I knew it when I'm developing.

Recently, I had been used Firestore when I make small web applications. Because there are no services cheap and large capacity. But when I found PlanetScale, I tried to change DB to it. Because it has free plan and large capacity. I like MySQL than NoSQL so I'm so excited.

I think I always use PlanetScale when I make somethings.

Next.js

Made by Next.js. API Server is also Next.js API Routes. So so so easy. There are nothing to think about client and server. Just make. If I work on job, it's not good to use API Routes. But on my indie hacking, I can't use others.

Using Internationalized Routing for English and Japanese localization.

Deploy

Using Cloud Run. Recently I'm using only this. So I'm now easy to develop with it.

By default, Cloud Run deploys when code pushed. But testing is easier than GitHub Actions, doing like this: push -> Test on GitHub Actions -> Build on Cloud Build (Starts with Webhook from Actions) -> Deploy to Cloud Run.

DB Migration is also executed on Cloud Build.

On testing, using simple API test by Jest and E2E test with Cypress. Cypress is very interesting. On Cypress Dashboard, you can see movie of testing.

Mail

Using SendGrid. Using Dynamic Templates. You don't need manage mail templates on your service.

And with receiving hook, saving received mail to DB. So you can send and receive mails only on web.

Flutter

Localization

Localization was complicated. When develop package, use example project created with package. When developing on it, there were no problems. But when I use the package in other app, localization was not worked well. Fixing was difficult.

Freezed

No problems. I could use.

Testing

Package is public. I can use GitHub Actions with no limit. So I write some tests.

Small story

Contact form on 'Contact Nite' is using its contact form API.
item.author: dala00

item.title: 使用 React 将文件上传到 Firebase 存储 | Uploading files to Firebase Storage using React
item.description:
阿舒托什·夏尔马

发表于在本文结束时，您将学习如何：

  • 创建并设置 Firebase 帐户
  • 将您的应用添加到 Firebase
  • 在 Firebase 中创建存储空间
  • 设置存储访问策略
  • 使用 React 应用程序上传照片

你应该已经知道的

  • 熟悉编写 React

我们将分三个步骤设置 Firebase：

  1. 创建一个新项目
  2. 存储和策略设置
  3. 连接的应对配置

要创建 Firebase 帐户，请访问https://firebase.google.com/ ，您可以使用 Google 帐户登录。 登录后使用转到控制台链接打开控制台或打开https://console.firebase.google.com/

您可以选择添加项目：
提供创建新项目所需的详细信息

设置谷歌分析，对于这个演示我们不需要这个。

单击创建项目后，几秒钟后项目就可以使用了。

点击存储
单击开始。 对于这个演示，我们将使用测试模式。 选择要存储文件的区域：

几秒钟后，您的存储空间将准备就绪。

为此，请转到项目概述，现在我们必须向该项目添加一个应用程序。
单击Web App，选择一个名称并注册应用程序。

您将看到在 Web 项目中使用 Firebase 的步骤。 复制并保存以备后用。

创建反应应用

现在让我们开始构建我们的 React 应用程序，您可以参考https://reactjs.org/docs/create-a-new-react-app.html来创建 React 应用程序的基本结构。

  • 使用以下命令安装 firebase 依赖项： npm install firebase

  • 现在，我们将 Firebase 配置导入项目。 创建一个文件 src\config\firebase.js 。 在此文件中导入 Firebase 组件
    import { initializeApp } from "firebase/app";
    import { getStorage } from "firebase/storage";

  • 复制我们从 firebase 控制台复制的 firebase 配置对象并导出存储常量
    export const app = initializeApp(firebaseConfig); export const storage = getStorage(app);
    这个存储变量将允许我们上传和下载 firebase 存储中的文件。

  • 在 App 组件或任何子组件中创建一个 Input 类型的文件和一个上传文件的按钮：
    <input type="file" onChange={onFileChange} />
    <button onClick={onFileUpload}>Upload!</button>

为了编写将文件上传到的过程，我们需要从 firebase 库中导入存储变量和一些组件。

import { ref, uploadBytesResumable, getDownloadURL } from '@firebase/storage';
import { storage } from '../config/firebase';

  • 即使单击按钮，我们也会创建一个存储引用，该引用将保存 Firebase 服务器上的存储和文件位置。

我们将使用uploadBytesResumable上传文件并使用getDownloadURL 从服务器获取URL，我们可以通过该URL 下载/查看上传的文件。 它还允许您监控和管理上传。

const uploadTask = uploadBytesResumable(storageRef, file);

事件 state_changed 可用于管理上传和查看当前状态。

  • 现在我们都完成了启动应用程序并尝试上传文件。

您可以在此处参考代码： https ://github.com/ashusharmatech/react-firebase-storage-demo

瓦利德·尼哈尔·艾哈迈德 - May 8 5月8日



原文:

Ashutosh Sharma

Posted on May 8

Firebase Cloud Storage is designed to help quickly and easily store and serve user-generated content, such as photos and videos.

By the end of this post you will learn how to :

  • Create and setup a firebase account
  • Add your app to firebase
  • Create Storage space in firebase
  • Setup policies for storage access
  • Upload photos using React Application

What you should already know

  • Familiarity with writing React

Firebase Setup

We will setup the Firebase in three steps:

  1. Firebase Account Creation
  2. Creation of a new Project
  3. Storage & Policy Setup
  4. Coping configurations for connection

- Firebase Account Creation:

To create Firebase account go to https://firebase.google.com/, you can sign in using the Google Account. Once logged in open the console using Go to console link or open https://console.firebase.google.com/

- Creation of a new Project

You will have an option to add a project:
Provide the required details to create a new project

Set Google Analytics, for this demo we don't need this.

Once you click on Create Project, in a few seconds project will be ready to use.

- Storage & Policy Setup

Click on Storage
Click on Get Started. Firebase will request you to select the security rules, if you are using this for development purposes you can select the Test mode which will allow you to access the data without authentication. For this demo, we will use Test mode. ( This is not recommended for production environments.)

Select the region where you want to store the files:

After a few seconds, your storage space will be ready.

- Coping configurations for connection

Firebase is all setup now we just have to copy the firebase config which will be required to connect to storage from react application. For this go to project overview and now we have to add an application to this project.
Click on Web App, choose a name and register app.

You will be presented with the steps to use Firebase in Web Project. Copy it and save it for later use.

Create React App

Let's now start with building our React Application, you can refer https://reactjs.org/docs/create-a-new-react-app.html to create a basic structure of React App.

  • Install firebase dependency using below command: npm install firebase

  • Now, we will import the Firebase configs to the project. Create a file src\config\firebase.js . Import the Firebase components in this file
    import { initializeApp } from "firebase/app";
    import { getStorage } from "firebase/storage";

  • Copy the firebase config object we copied from firebase console and export storage constant
    export const app = initializeApp(firebaseConfig); export const storage = getStorage(app);
    This storage variable will allow us to upload and download the files in firebase storage.

  • In App component or any subcomponent create a Input type file and a button to upload file:
    <input type="file" onChange={onFileChange} />
    <button onClick={onFileUpload}>Upload!</button>

For writing the process for uploading the files to we need to import the storage variable and few components from firebase library.

import { ref, uploadBytesResumable, getDownloadURL } from '@firebase/storage';
import { storage } from '../config/firebase';

  • On button's click even we will create a storage reference which will hold the storage and file location at firebase server.

const storageRef = ref(storage,/files/${file.name});

  • We will use uploadBytesResumable to upload the file and getDownloadURL to get the URL from server by which we can download/view the uploaded file. uploadBytesResumable will return UploadTask which represents the process of uploading an object. It also allows you to monitor and manage the upload.

const uploadTask = uploadBytesResumable(storageRef, file);

The event state_changed can be used to manage the upload and view the current state.

  • Now we are all done start the application and try uploading file.

Github:

You can refer the code here : https://github.com/ashusharmatech/react-firebase-storage-demo

Waleedh Nihal Ahmed - May 8
item.author: ashusharmatech

item.title: 将 React 路由器从 V5 升级到 V6 | Upgrading React Router From V5 to V6
item.description:
几周前，当我尝试处理一个旧项目时，我在更新我的开发包时遇到了一个错误，因为我的 React Router 更新导致了重大更改。 我尝试过阅读文档，但有时您知道它们可能很长而且很乏味，所以我决定写这篇文章来帮助那些也在尝试更新他们的项目的人。

最后，升级非常简单，没有太大变化，只是代码差异很小。 版本 6 比版本 5 好得多，也更容易理解，如果你可以升级，你应该升级。

所以首先你可以在 vscode 中打开你的旧项目并安装 react-router 版本 6，通过运行 npm install react-router-dom@6 来确保你安装最新版本 6，这就是我们可以在这里做的：

npm install react-router-dom@6

当您安装 react-router 版本 6 时。 尝试在终端运行 npm start，你会得到一个编译失败的错误（开关不是从 react-router-dom 导出的）。

这给我们带来了第一个变化。 当我们使用 react-router v5 时，我们从 react-router 包中导入 switch 组件来包装我们的路由，但在 react-router v6 中，我们使用 routes 组件来包装路由组件。

而且只有 Route 组件可以是 Routes 的子组件，任何其他组件都会导致错误。

另一个重要的变化是您的组件不应再是您的路由组件的子组件，而是路由接受一个元素道具，然后您将一个动态值传递给元素，该动态值是将组件呈现为 JSX。

最后请注意，动态路由仍然可以正常工作（添加了一些新功能，但工作方式相同）。

感谢您的阅读，请对您想要添加的任何更正或补充发表评论。
升级文档链接



原文:

React Router version 6 was released some months it is really important because React Router is one of the most used React packages.

React itself doesn't come with a router package so majority of React projects from small single page apps to big organizations use React Router.

Some weeks ago while I was trying to work on an old project I ran into an error when I updated my development packages because of my React Router update that caused breaking changes. I tried going through the documentation but sometimes you know they can be long and tedious, so I decided to write this post to help anyone who is also trying to update their project.

In the end, it’s quite simple to upgrade and not much has changed, just little code difference. Version 6 is a lot better and easier to understand than version 5, if you can upgrade, you should.

So first you can open your old project in vscode and install react-router version 6, do this by running npm install react-router-dom@6 which ensures that you install the latest version 6 and that’s what we can do here:

npm install react-router-dom@6

When you get react-router version 6 installed. Try running npm start in the terminal, you will get a failed to compile error(switch is not exported from react-router-dom).

This brings us to the first change. When we used react-router v5, we imported the switch component from the react-router package to wrap our routes, but instead in react-router v6 we use the routes component to wrap the route component.

N.B You still have to import BrowserRouter from react-router-dom to wrap your root app component. And also only Route component can be a child of Routes any other component will lead to an error.

Another important change is that your component should no longer be a child of your route component but instead the route takes in an element prop and then you pass a dynamic value to element and that dynamic value is that to be rendered component as JSX.

And lastly note that dynamic routing still works as you normal(some new features were added but it works the same).

Thanks for reading and please comment on any corrections or additions you will like to add.
Link to upgrade documentation
item.author: jumbo02

item.title: TestNG 测试框架 - 简介 | TestNG testing framework - Introduction
item.description:
什么是测试框架？

测试框架是一组用于创建和设计测试用例的指南或规则。 框架由旨在帮助 QA 专业人员更有效地测试的实践和工具的组合组成。

流行的可用选项：

为什么选择 TestNG？

注释。

  • 在任意大的线程池中运行您的测试，并使用各种可用的策略（线程中的所有方法，每个测试类一个线程，等等）。
  • 测试您的代码是否是多线程安全的。
  • 灵活的测试配置。
  • 支持数据驱动测试（使用@DataProvider）。
  • 支持参数。
  • 强大的执行模型（不再有 TestSuite）。
  • 支持多种工具和插件（Eclipse、IDEA、Maven 等）。
  • 嵌入 BeanShell 以获得更大的灵活性。
  • 用于运行时和日志记录的默认 JDK 函数（无依赖性）。
  • 应用服务器测试的依赖方法。 断言

    预期结果：测试应用程序时某个时间点的预期结果。

  • 实际结果：测试应用程序时某个时间点的实际结果。

例子：

 Assert.assertEquals(actualOutcome,expectedOutcome);

断言的类型

硬断言：硬断言是那些在断言语句失败时停止测试执行的断言，因此后续的断言语句不会被验证。
软断言：在软断言中，即使一个断言验证失败，后续断言也会继续运行，即测试执行不会停止。

例子：

 # Hard Assert Assert.assertEquals(actualResult,expectedResult); # Soft Assert SoftAssert softassert = new SoftAssert(); softassert.assertEquals(actualResult, expecetedResult); softassert.assertAll();

示例用法

代码

只有@test注释的方法被称为 test 应该包含测试脚本，而@BeforeMethod/@AfterMethod方法应该用于为每个测试设置先决条件和清理。

 import org.testng.annotations.AfterTest; import org.testng.annotations.BeforeTest; import org.testng.annotations.Test; public class Runner { @BeforeTest public void beforeTest(){ System.out.println("method to be executed before test"); } @Test public void test(){ System.out.println("test method"); } @AfterTest public void afterTest(){ System.out.println("method to be executed after test"); } }

输出：

 method to be executed before test test method method to be executed after test =============================================== Default Suite Total tests run: 1, Passes: 1, Failures: 0, Skips: 0 ===============================================

几个有用的网址：

TestNG webpage TestNG 网页
Javatpoint tutorial Javatpoint 教程



原文:

What is a Testing Framework?

A testing framework is a set of guidelines or rules used for creating and designing test cases. A framework is comprised of a combination of practices and tools that are designed to help QA professionals test more efficiently.

Popular available options:

Why TestNG?

TestNG is a testing framework inspired by JUnit and NUnit but introduces some new functionalities that make it more powerful and easier to use, such as:

Annotations.

  • Run your tests in arbitrarily big thread pools with various policies available (all methods in their thread, one thread per test class, etc…).
  • Test that your code is multithread safe.
  • Flexible test configuration.
  • Support for data-driven testing (with @DataProvider).
  • Support for parameters.
  • Powerful execution model (no more TestSuite).
  • Supported by a variety of tools and plug-ins (Eclipse, IDEA, Maven, etc…).
  • Embeds BeanShell for further flexibility.
  • Default JDK functions for runtime and logging (no dependencies).
  • Dependent methods for application server testing. TestNG is designed to cover all categories of tests: unit, functional, end-to-end, integration, etc…

Annotations in TestNG

Assertions

Assertions in TestNG are a way to verify that the expected result and the actual result match or not.

  • Expected Result: expected outcome at a point in time while testing application.
  • Actual Result: actual outcome at a point in time while testing application.

example:

Assert.assertEquals(actualOutcome,expectedOutcome);

Types Of Assertions

Hard Assertion: Hard Asserts are those asserts that stop the test execution when an assert statement fails, and the subsequent assert statements are therefore not validated.
Soft Assertions: In soft asserts, the subsequent assertions keep on running even though one assert validation fails, i.e., the test execution does not stop.

Example:

# Hard Assert
Assert.assertEquals(actualResult,expectedResult);
# Soft Assert
SoftAssert softassert = new SoftAssert();
softassert.assertEquals(actualResult, expecetedResult);
softassert.assertAll();

Example Usage

pom.xml

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.testng</groupId>
    <artifactId>testng</artifactId>
    <version>7.5</version>
</dependency>

code

only @test annotated methods are called test should hold test scripts, whereas @BeforeMethod/@AfterMethod methods should be used for setting up prerequisites & cleaning up for each tests.

import org.testng.annotations.AfterTest;
import org.testng.annotations.BeforeTest;
import org.testng.annotations.Test;

public class Runner {
    @BeforeTest
    public void beforeTest(){
        System.out.println("method to be executed before test");
    }

    @Test
    public void test(){
        System.out.println("test method");
    }

    @AfterTest
    public void afterTest(){
        System.out.println("method to be executed after test");
    }
}

output:

method to be executed before test
test method
method to be executed after test
===============================================
Default Suite
Total tests run: 1, Passes: 1, Failures: 0, Skips: 0
===============================================

Few useful URLs:

TestNG webpage
Javatpoint tutorial
item.author: arvind_choudhary

item.title: 将 WordPress 应用程序部署到 Amazon Lightsail | Deploy WordPress App to Amazon Lightsail
item.description:
与大多数其他服务不同，它具有固定的每月定价，因此您不必担心每月的巨额账单。

它对WordPress 、Magento、Joomla、Prestashop、Drupal、Ghost 和 Django 应用程序提供开箱即用的支持。

它提供了预配置的堆栈，例如 LAMP、NGINX、MEAN 和 Node.js。 只需单击几下，您还可以启动预配置的 Plesk、WHM 和 cPanel。

但这还不是全部。 您可以启动任何基于 Linux 和 Windows 操作系统的实例。 我个人最喜欢的是Ubuntu和Amazon Linux 2 。

先决条件

需要一个有效的 AWS 账户来部署 WP 应用程序。 如果您没有，只需从此处创建一个。 在开始部署您的应用程序之前，请阅读有关Amazon Lightsail 定价和免费套餐的信息。 不用担心，我会尽量将所有内容都保留在免费套餐内。 在撰写本文时，您可以免费运行一个 Lightsail 实例 3 个月。

安装 WordPress 应用程序

如果尚未登录，请登录您的 AWS 账户。 转到Lightsail 主页。 你应该看到这样的页面：

你看到这个了吗？ 我们走吧🏃‍♂️

创建实例

  1. 现在单击大的创建实例按钮。 如果您没有看到，请按照以下说明操作：

  2. 让我们选择Instance location 、 platform和blueprint ：
    我建议选择离客户更近的位置。

    不是多站点之一。

  • 现在向下滚动到选择您的实例计划部分。 我选择了一个免费套餐并且只有一个实例。 这是我的设置：

  • 最后滚动到页面底部并单击Create instance按钮。 等待状态变为Running 。 恭喜，您的实例已启动🚀

    • 分配静态 IP（可选）

    每次停止和启动实例时都会发生变化。 当我启动实例时，我的公共 IP 是这样的：

    停止并启动实例后，它获得了一个新的公共 IP 地址：

    我们必须解决这个问题。 因此，让我们创建并分配一个静态 IP ：

    1. 单击您的应用程序实例名称以查看您的实例的详细信息。
    2. 单击+ 从此处创建静态 IP ：
    3. 添加一个有意义的名称，然后单击创建： 您需要为未附加到正在运行的实例的静态 IP 付费。

    使用 SSH 连接到实例

    您可以通过两种方式连接到实例：

    使用浏览器

    这是最简单的。 转到“连接”选项卡，然后单击“使用 SSH 连接”：

    使用 SSH 客户端

    1. 从这里下载 SSH 密钥：
    2. 如果您使用 PuTTY，您可以按照本教程进行操作。
    3. 如果您在 macOS 或 Linux 中使用终端，请在下载 SSH 密钥的位置运行此命令。
     # Run for the first time to fix the permission chmod 400 <path/to/your-lightsail-ssh-key>.pem # Connect to your Lightsail instance # Username and Public IP can be found in "Connect" tab ssh -i "<path/to/your-lightsail-key>.pem" <username>@<public_ip>

    获取管理员用户名和密码

    1. 您可以从“连接”选项卡中获取用户名。 用户名是此堆栈的用户。
    2. 要获取密码，请登录 SSH 并运行此命令
    cat bitnami_application_password

    1. 现在打开浏览器选项卡并转到http://<public_ip>/wp-admin 。 在上面输入用户名和密码。 您应该像我一样看到 WP Admin 仪表板。

    添加域

    1. 转到主页。 单击网络选项卡，然后单击创建 DNS 区域按钮
    2. 添加您的域名。 滚动到底部并单击创建 DNS 区域按钮
    3. 在您的域控制面板中添加这些名称服务器记录（例如 Namecheap、GoDaddy）。 你的记录可能与我的不同
    4. 现在到了好的部分。 单击+ 添加记录按钮。 添加您的实例并单击对勾图标进行保存。 现在更新wp_options表的siteurl和home行中的域名。

    导入现有应用

    导入数据库

    您可能有一个现有的数据库。 您可以将数据库导入 Lightsail 实例。 您可以使用 TablePlus、MySQL Workbench 等数据库客户端应用程序，甚至只是终端。

    您的数据库凭据存在于wp-config.php文件中。 通过 SSH 连接到您的实例并运行以下命令：

     cat stack/wordpress/wp-config.php

    使用数据库凭证连接数据库，并将localhost替换为实例的公共 IP。 通过 SSH 连接到您的数据库（推荐）或在此处添加3306端口：

    导入现有文件

    位于stack/wordpress中的应用程序文件夹。 您可以通过两种方式上传文件：

    1. 使用相同的 SSH 凭据上传文件。
    2. 使用以下命令安装 git：

    现在使用 git clone/pull 命令从 git 中提取文件。

    结论

    这只是将 WordPress 应用程序部署到 Amazon Lightsail 实例的第一步。 你可以做的还有很多。 欢迎您提出问题、给我反馈或任何改进要求。



    原文:

    AWS Lightsail can be the easiest entry to the AWS Cloud. It has fixed monthly pricing unlike most other services, so you don't have to worry about a huge monthly bill.

    Lightsail is really great for small to medium apps. It has out-of-the-box support for WordPress, Magento, Joomla, Prestashop, Drupal, Ghost, and Django apps.

    It provides pre-configured stacks like LAMP, NGINX, MEAN, and Node.js. You can also launch pre-configured Plesk, WHM & cPanel in just a few clicks.

    But that's not all. You can launch any Linux-based and Windows OS instance. My personal favorites are Ubuntu and Amazon Linux 2.

    Prerequisites

    Need a valid AWS account to deploy the WP app. If you don't have one, just create one from here. Before starting to deploy your app read about Amazon Lightsail Pricing and Free-Tier. Don't worry, I will try to keep everything within the free tier. At this time of writing, you can run a Lightsail instance for free for 3 months.

    Install WordPress App

    Login to your AWS account if not already. Go to Lightsail homepage. You should see a page like this:

    Are you seeing this? Let's go 🏃‍♂️

    Create Instance

    1. Now click on the big Create Instance button. If you don't see one, follow this:

    2. Let's choose Instance location, platform, and blueprint:
      A. Click the link to change location and availability zone (AZ) if not what you want. I recommend selecting a location closer to your customers.

      B. Choose the Linux/Unix platform.


      C. Select the Apps + OS from the blueprint.
      D. Now choose WordPress from the app list. Not the multisite one.

  • Now scroll down to Choose your instance plan section. I chose a free-tier plan and only one instance. Here is my setup:

  • Finally scroll to the bottom of the page and click the Create instance button. Wait for the status to become Running. Congratulations, your instance has launched 🚀

    • Assign a Static IP (optional)

    AWS Lightsail Instance comes with public (and private) IP. Which changes every time Stop and Start the instance. My Public IP was this when I launched the instance:

    After Stop and Start the instance, it got a new Public IP address:

    We have to fix that. So, let's create and assign a Static IP:

    1. Click on your application instance name to see the details of your instance.
    2. Click on + Create static IP from here:
    3. Add a meaningful name and click Create: > ⚠️ Warning: You are allowed to assign 5 static IPs for free. You will be charged for static IP that is not attached to a running instance.

    Connect to the instance using SSH

    You can connect to an instance in two ways:

    Using browser

    That's the easiest. Go to the Connect tab and click Connect using SSH:

    Using SSH Client

    1. Download the SSH key from here:
    2. If you are using PuTTY you can follow this tutorial.
    3. If you are using a terminal in macOS or Linux, run this command on where you downloaded the SSH key. 
    # Run for the first time to fix the permission
chmod 400 <path/to/your-lightsail-ssh-key>.pem # Connect to your Lightsail instance
# Username and Public IP can be found in "Connect" tab
ssh -i "<path/to/your-lightsail-key>.pem" <username>@<public_ip>

    Get Admin Username and Password

    1. You can get the username from the Connect tab. The username is user for this stack.
    2. To get the password, log in to SSH and run this command 
    cat bitnami_application_password

    1. Now open a browser tab and go to http://<public_ip>/wp-admin. Enter username and password above. You should see the WP Admin dashboard like me.

    Add a Domain

    1. Go to the Home page. Click on the Networking tab and then hit Create DNS zone button
    2. Add your domain name. Scroll to the bottom and click the Create DNS zone button
    3. Add these name server records in your domain control panel (ex. Namecheap, GoDaddy). You might have different records than mine
    4. Now to the good part. Click + Add Record button. Add your instance and click on tick icon to save. > ⚠️ It may take few minutes to days to propagate DNS records.
    5. Now update the domain name in siteurl and home rows of wp_options table.

    Import Existing App

    Import Database

    You may have an existing database. You can import your database to the Lightsail instance. You can use a Database client app like TablePlus, MySQL Workbench, or even just the terminal.

    Your database credentials exist in wp-config.php file. Connect to your instance via SSH and run this command:

    cat stack/wordpress/wp-config.php

    Use DB credentials to connect to DB, and replace localhost with the instance's public IP. Connect to your DB via SSH (recommended) or add the 3306 port here:

    ℹ️ Don't forget to change the domain name in siteurl and home rows of wp_options table.

    Import existing files

    Application folder located in stack/wordpress. You can upload files in two ways:

    1. SFTP Client: You can use SFTP clients like FileZilla or Transmit to upload files. Use the same SSH credential to upload files.
    2. Git: Connect to your instance via SSH. Install git by using this command:

    Now pull your files from git using the git clone/pull command.

    Conclusion

    This is just the first step toward deploying your WordPress app to Amazon Lightsail instance. There are lot more you can do. You are welcome to ask your questions, give me feedback, or any improvement request.
item.author: harunrrayhan

item.title: 无服务器 Gitlab Runner #Part1 | Serverless Gitlab Runner #Part1
item.description:
我来自系统管理员背景，多年来我一直在夜间和周末支持基础架构和应用程序。 如今，借助 Cloud，您可以轻松构建需要很少或零维护的解决方案。

所以今天我想谈谈我们如何使用 Fargate 在 AWS ECS 集群上实现 Serverless Gitlab 运行器解决方案。 这是我解释这个概念的第一部分，以及一个展示如何实现它的图表。 接下来，我将展示一些代码，一旦准备好，我将在 Github 上发布。

什么？

让我们从几个问题开始：什么是 gitlab runner？ 什么是 Fargate？

它从 gitlab 获取代码，从.gitlab-ci.yml获取配置并执行它。 它可以是虚拟机或 docker 容器。 您可以使用 Gitlab 提供的 Shared runners 或托管您自己的 runners。 我不是在这里深入解释 gitlab 管道如何工作以及如何配置.gitlab-ci.yml ，如果你在这里，你可能已经知道并且可能比我更好:)

作为托管服务意味着您不需要做任何事情，AWS 会为您管理配置和补丁。 对于 ECS，您需要创建应用程序在集群上运行所需的配置，例如内存、cpu、网络和 docker 映像。 这些配置称为任务定义。 再说一遍，你们都知道 ECS 是如何工作的，对吧？ 为什么？

在什么之后？ 总有为什么？ 那么为什么我们要构建这样的解决方案，为什么要在 ECS 上而不是在 EKS 上呢？ 好问题

好吧，首先我们这样做是为了好玩，为什么不呢！ 第二个 ECS 比 EKS 更容易一些。 您无需在 EC2 实例上手动安装 k8s。

使用 ECS，您只需配置任务定义并完成。 设置集群和运行应用程序需要较少的专业知识。

另一方面，Fargate 是 AWS 托管计算引擎。 您不需要预置任何实例，AWS 会为您管理它。 这意味着更少的扩展、修补、保护和管理服务器的运营开销。 在这里您还可以找到如何在 EKS 上运行跑步者。

如何？

现在来了一个有趣的问题：我们如何构建它？

这是一张图

如您所见，一切主要由少数 lambda 函数管理。 但是让我解释一下流程是如何工作的。

我们需要的是带有 Fargate 驱动程序的 Gitlab runner executor 的 Docker 映像，以及随后由 executor 执行的 runner 作业的一些其他映像。

  1. 首先，您需要在存储库中创建一个 webhook，其端点是一个 lambda，传递将由 runner 用于自动注册到 gitlab 的令牌。 当您配置令牌时，您还会传递将用于身份验证的密钥。 根据您的需要，您可以在查询字符串中传递更多参数。
  2. 下一个 lambda 从 EventBridge 获取事件，从 SSM 读取一些参数（如运行器令牌）并检查是否有其他运行器在运行。 如果一切顺利，它会启动 gitlab runner executor，传递令牌和更多信息。
  3. 跑步者在 Gitlab 上注册，它现在可以接受工作了。
  4. 当触发新管道时，运行器在单独的容器中启动新作业。
  5. 当我向您展示代码时，请在第 2 部分中详细介绍。

    问题

    这个解决方案非常好，尽管如果您有一些复杂的管道，它并不完美，因为 taskDefinition 中有一个限制，不允许覆盖管道中的作业图像参数。 这意味着您需要更大的 Docker 镜像来管理不同的案例，这并不理想。

    参考

    结论

    我知道这个解决方案并不理想，但我喜欢尝试甚至改进已有的解决方案。 我相信 Gitlab 和 AWS 都会很快解决镜像问题。

    我希望你喜欢这篇文章并给你一些启发，希望我能尽快在 Github 中推送一些代码，以便在下一部分与你分享。

    现在感谢您停下来阅读它，我很高兴“听到”您的想法，特别是如果您已经实施了它。

    再见！



原文:

I come from a sysadmin background and Ive spent many years supporting infrastructures and applications during nights and weekends. Nowadays with Cloud you can easily build solutions that requires little or zero maintenance.

So today I want to talk about how we can implement a Serverless Gitlab runner solution on a AWS ECS Cluster using Fargate. This is the first part where I explain the concept and a diagram that shows how to implement it. Next I will show some code I will post on Github as soon as its ready.

What?

Lets start with a couple of questions: What is a gitlab runner? And What is Fargate?

A Gitlab runner is nothing more that an application that execute Gitlab jobs in pipelines. It gets the code from gitlab and the configuration from .gitlab-ci.yml and execute it. It can be a virtual machine or a docker container. You can use Shared runners provided by Gitlab or host your own runners. Im not here to go deep and explain how gitlab pipeline works and how to configure .gitlab-ci.yml, if you are here you probably know already and probably better than me :)

Fargate is an AWS managed service where you can run your ECS or EKS cluster. Being s managed service means that you dont need to do anything, AWS manages it for you, configuration and patches. In case of ECS what you need is create configurations that are needed by your application to run on the cluster, like memory, cpu, network and the docker image. These configurations are called task definition. Again, you all know how ECS works, right? :)

Why?

After the What? there is always the Why?. So why we want to build such solution and why on ECS and not on EKS? Good question

Well for start we do it for fun, why not! And second ECS is a bit easier than EKS. ECS is the AWS container orchestrator, it's a proprietary solution. EKS is basically kubernetes on AWS. You don't need to install manually k8s on EC2 instances.

With ECS you just need to configure a task definition and its done. It requires less expertise to setup a cluster and run your application.

On the other hand Fargate is the AWS managed compute engine. You don't need to provision any instance, AWS manages it for you. That means less operational overhead of scaling, patching, securing, and managing servers. Fargate is compatible with both ECS and EKS.

Here you can find also how to run runners on EKS.

HOW?

Now comes the fun question: how can we build this?

Heres a diagram

As you can see everything is mainly managed by few lambda functions. But let me explain how the flow works.

What we need is a Docker image for the Gitlab runner executor with Fargate drivers and some other images for the runner jobs which are then executed by the executor.

  1. First you need to create a webhook in your repository which endpoint is a lambda, passing the token that will be used by the runner to register automatically to gitlab. When you configure the token you pass also the secret that will be used for authentication. Depends on your needs you can pass more parameters in the query strings.
  2. The webhook lambda authenticates the call against a predefined secrete we have storer in SSM Parameter Store and put an event to EventBridge.
  3. An EventBridge rule filters the event and call another lambda that will start a runner with Fargate drivers.
  4. The next lambda get the event from EventBridge, read some parameters from SSM (like the runner token) and check if there are other runners running. If all is good it starts the gitlab runner executor passing the token and few more information.
  5. The runner register against Gitlab and it's now ready to accept jobs.
  6. When a new pipeline is triggered the runner starts new jobs in separate containers.
  7. An event on EventBridge is also scheduled to run every tot time to check if the runner is doing something (if there are jobs running), and if not it will stop the runner to save money.

Well go more into the details on part 2 when Ill show you the code.

The problem

This solution is quite nice, although if you have some complex pipelines it's not perfect as there is a limitation in the taskDefinition that won't allow to override the job image parameter in a pipeline. That means you need bigger Docker images to manage different cases, which is not ideal.

References

https://docs.gitlab.com/runner/configuration/runner\_autoscale\_aws\_fargate/

Conclusion

I know this solution is not ideal, but I like experimenting and even improve what's already there. I'm sure both Gitlab and AWS will solve the problem of the image soon.

I hope you liked this article and gave you some inspiration and hopefully I can push some code in Github soon to share with you in the next part.

For now thanks for stopping by and reading it and I'm happy to "hear" your thought especially if you have already implemented it.

Ciao!
item.author: sgrilux_41

item.title: 巢JS 101 | NestJS 101
item.description:
文章主要介绍 NestJS，框架 Typescript 后端的开发者 des API。

索梅尔

安装 de NestJS 和初始化 de l'application

$ mkdir library_nest && cd library_nest

通过 un navigateur 在 peut accéder à http://localhost:3000/上。

控制器/服务/实体

在 la structure suivante de src上：

 src └───book ├───controller └───service
 $ ls -R src | awk ' /:$/&&f{s=$0;f=0} /:$/&&!f{sub(/:$/,"");s=$0;f=1;next} NF&&f{ print s"/"$0 }' src/app.controller.spec.ts src/app.controller.ts src/app.module.ts src/app.service.ts src/book/ src/main.ts src/book/book.entity.spec.ts src/book/book.entity.ts src/book/book.module.ts src/book/controller/ src/book/service/ src/book/controller/book.controller.spec.ts src/book/controller/book.controller.ts src/book/service/book.service.spec.ts src/book/service/book.service.ts

可使用的修改文件

结论

Cette introduction permet de comprendre l'architecture de base de NestJS, dans des prochains articles, nous nous attaquerons à l'accès à une base de données avec la librairie TypeORM. Cette 介绍 permet de comprendre l'architecture de base de NestJS、dans des prochains 文章、nous nous attaquerons à l'accès à une base de données avec la librairie TypeORM。

Retrouvez les sources sur https://github.com/zorky/library_nestjs/tree/nestjs-101 Retrouvez 文件来源 sur https://github.com/zorky/library_nestjs/tree/nestjs-101



原文:

Article pour introduire NestJS, framework Typescript backend pour développer des API.

Sommaire

NestJS est un framework Javascript (plus précisément TypeScript) pour développer un backend au-dessus de NodeJs et Express ou Fastify (qui gérons les routes de bas niveau HTTP).

La philosophie se rapproche de frameworks backends de type ASPNET WebApi C# / .NET, Java Spring ou Angular côté frontend : approche par couches (controllers, services via de l'injection de dépendances, qui va apporter une couche d'abstraction), par attributs / annotations que l'on appellera décorateurs que l'on retrouve en Angular, modulaire (comme Angular). L'écriture du code s'effectue en TypeScript (mais Javascript reste possible), on retrouvera nos bonnes pratiques de développement (typage, POO, design patterns).

NestJS propose également un CLI nest qui permettra de générer du scaffolding de classes, services, controllers, etc, bon nombre de modules / plugins / mécanismes : des ORM avec par exemple TypeORM ou d'authentification avec PassportJS (et la mécanique technique de gestion de JWT) et d'autorisation : qui a le droit à accéder à telle ou telle API / fonction (Guards avec des rôles, comme sous Angular), de sécurité avec CORS et une prise en charge d'OpenAPI via Swagger pour la documentation de vos APIs.

NestJs d'un point de vue architecture reprend tout ce que l'on connaît déjà de frameworks backend ou frontend de type SPA (Angular, React, VueJs), en TypeScript, ce qui permet de mutualiser ses connaissances front côté back.

Nous allons reprendre dans cette introduction, l'application de gestion de livres de la série Angular / Django REST, en ne faisant que les livres pour l'instant, sans authentification, et sans interface, en axant uniquement sur une API.

Installation de NestJS et initialisation de l'application

$ mkdir library_nest && cd library_nest

Commençons par le CLI, de façon global :

$ npm i -g @nestjs/cli

Création du squelette de l'application que l'on appellera backend grâce au CLI, je choisis npm comme gestionnaire de packages, aller dans backend à l'issue du scaffolding

$ nest new backend
⚡ We will scaffold your app in a few seconds.. CREATE backend/.eslintrc.js (665 bytes)
CREATE backend/.prettierrc (51 bytes)
CREATE backend/nest-cli.json (118 bytes)
CREATE backend/package.json (1992 bytes)
CREATE backend/README.md (3340 bytes)
CREATE backend/tsconfig.build.json (97 bytes)
CREATE backend/tsconfig.json (546 bytes)
CREATE backend/src/app.controller.spec.ts (617 bytes)
CREATE backend/src/app.controller.ts (274 bytes)
CREATE backend/src/app.module.ts (249 bytes)
CREATE backend/src/app.service.ts (142 bytes)
CREATE backend/src/main.ts (208 bytes)
CREATE backend/test/app.e2e-spec.ts (630 bytes)
CREATE backend/test/jest-e2e.json (183 bytes) ? Which package manager would you ❤️ to use? npm
🚀 Successfully created project backend
👉 Get started with the following commands: $ cd backend

On peut lancer le serveur

$ npm run start ou $ npm run start:dev pour la prise en compte des modifications de code et du rechargement automatique du serveur (le watcher), en mode développement, c'est plus que recommandé.

La compilation / transpilation en javascript est effectuée dans dist qu'utilisera le serveur.

On peut accéder à http://localhost:3000/ via un navigateur.

S'il se trouvait un souci avec le port (déjà pris par exemple), il est possible de le modifier dans le fichier backend/src/main.ts

import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
import { AppModule } from './app.module'; async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule); await app.listen(3000); // modifier le port ICI
}
bootstrap();

Controller / service / entité

Dans cet article, nous ferons simple, pour démontrer l'architecture controller / service, sans accès à une base de données.

Le déroulement des appels peut être schématisé de la manière suivante :

(source : https://excalidraw.com/#json=sErd2_EvBGrB4K5GD8HfV,kjKwMJmGzsk-i5md3k67zA)

Créons avec le CLI quelques squelettes, dans un module book.

Nous aurons dans ce 1er article, un module book dans lequel se trouvera un controller bookControler et un service bookService et une classe entité book. Dans cet article, on n'accédera pas à une base de données, les livres seront juste une liste en dur dans le code.

$ nest g module book # il crée src/book/book.module.ts et met à jour le module principal src/app.module.ts qui le référence
$ nest generate --flat controller book/controller/book # controller en mode flat pour générer les fichiers dans un emplacement choisi, ici book/controller/book, il met aussi à jour le module book.module.ts
$ nest generate --flat service book/service/book # idem mais pour le service, le module book.module.ts est mis à jour
$ nest g --flat class book/book.entity # la classe entité

On a la structure suivante de src :

src
└───book
    ├───controller
    └───service

$ ls -R src | awk '
/:$/&&f{s=$0;f=0}
/:$/&&!f{sub(/:$/,"");s=$0;f=1;next}
NF&&f{ print s"/"$0 }'

src/app.controller.spec.ts
src/app.controller.ts
src/app.module.ts
src/app.service.ts
src/book/
src/main.ts
src/book/book.entity.spec.ts
src/book/book.entity.ts
src/book/book.module.ts
src/book/controller/
src/book/service/
src/book/controller/book.controller.spec.ts
src/book/controller/book.controller.ts
src/book/service/book.service.spec.ts
src/book/service/book.service.ts

Modifications des fichiers pour le rendre utilisable

L'entité book devient :

export class BookEntity { id: number; title: string;
}

Le service renvoie une liste en dur de livres ou un livre en particulier par son ID, il sera injecté dans le controller, le mot clé @Injectable est nécessaire, on initialise quelques livres en dur dans le service :

import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { BookEntity } from '../book.entity'; @Injectable()
export class BookService { books: BookEntity[] = [ <BookEntity>{ id: 1, title: 'NestJS 101' }, <BookEntity>{ id: 2, title: 'Angular' }, ]; async getAll(): Promise<BookEntity[]> { return this.books; } async getById(id): Promise<BookEntity> { return this.books.find((_book: BookEntity) => _book.id === Number(id.id)); }
}

et le controller qui l'utilise, le constructeur, comme en Angular, permet d'injecter le service. Les décorateurs @Controller() et @Get() permettent de définir notre API : sur /api/books et la méthode GET via @Get() sur la fonction GetAll() qui renvoie tous les livres ou /api/books/2 GetById() sur un ID particulier.

import { Controller } from '@nestjs/common';
import { BookService } from '../service/book.service'; @Controller('api/books')
export class BookController { constructor(private bookService: BookService) {} @Get() async getAll(): Promise<BookEntity[]> { return await this.bookService.getAll(); } @Get(':id') async getById(@Param() id): Promise<BookEntity> { return await this.bookService.getById(id); }
}

Le serveur recharge le tout

[13:31:58] File change detected. Starting incremental compilation... [13:31:59] Found 0 errors. Watching for file changes. [Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [NestFactory] Starting Nest application...
[Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [InstanceLoader] AppModule dependencies initialized +95ms
[Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [InstanceLoader] BookModule dependencies initialized +2ms
[Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [RoutesResolver] BookController {/api/books}: +121ms
[Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [RouterExplorer] Mapped {/api/books, GET} route +7ms
[Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [RouterExplorer] Mapped {/api/books/:id, GET} route +2ms
[Nest] 3952 - 08/05/2022, 14:12:11 LOG [NestApplication] Nest application successfully started +4ms

et l'API renvoie bien tous les livres au format JSON

ou un des livres

Conclusion

Cette introduction permet de comprendre l'architecture de base de NestJS, dans des prochains articles, nous nous attaquerons à l'accès à une base de données avec la librairie TypeORM.

Retrouvez les sources sur https://github.com/zorky/library_nestjs/tree/nestjs-101
item.author: zorky

item.title: 使用 terraform 标记 | Tagging with terraform
item.description:
我们都知道，当我们在 AWS 中与不同账户共享资源时，标签不会与资源一起共享。 标签是基于帐户的，因此您还需要将标签分配给其他帐户。

所以让我们直接跳到一个例子。

您有一个与您的生产账户共享的 VPC 的网络账户。 您希望使用在网络帐户中提供的相同名称来命名您的 VPC。

首先您需要收集 VPC 数据（使用连接到网络帐户的提供程序）

 data "aws_vpc" "selected" { filter { name = "tag:Environment" values = ["production"] } provider = aws.central-networking }

然后，通过生产提供商，您可以标记您的 VPC

 resource "aws_ec2_tag" "my_prod_vpc" { resource_id = data.aws_vpc.selected.id key = "Name" value = data.aws_vpc.selected.tags.Name provider = aws.production }

您还可以使用 for_each 将多个标签分配给同一资源：

 resource "aws_ec2_tag" "my_prod_vpc" { for_each = local.tags resource_id = data.aws_vpc.selected.id key = each.key value = each.value }

就这样，轻松愉快！

我希望你喜欢它在下一篇文章中见。

再见！

此处了解有关 aws_ec2_tag 的更多信息



原文:

We all know that when we share a resource in AWS with a different account, tags are not shared alongside the resource. Tags are account based so you need to assign tags to the other account as well.

Terraform comes to help with aws_ec2_tag which allows us to tag indivvidual resources that are created outside Terraform.

So lets jump directly to an example.

You have a networking account with a VPC that you are sharing with your production account. You want to Name your VPC with the same name that has been given in the networking account.

first you need to collect VPC data (using a provider that connects to the networking account)

data "aws_vpc" "selected" {
  filter {
    name = "tag:Environment"
    values = ["production"]
  }

  provider = aws.central-networking
}

then, with a production provider, you can tag your VPC

resource "aws_ec2_tag" "my_prod_vpc" {
  resource_id = data.aws_vpc.selected.id
  key = "Name"
  value = data.aws_vpc.selected.tags.Name

  provider = aws.production
}

You can also use for_each to assign multiple tags to the same resource:

resource "aws_ec2_tag" "my_prod_vpc" {
  for_each = local.tags

  resource_id = data.aws_vpc.selected.id
  key = each.key
  value = each.value
}

Thats it, easy peasy!

I hope you have enjoyed itsee you on the next post.

CIAO!

Here for more information about aws_ec2_tag
