item.title: Web 浏览器的工作原理 - 创建可访问性树（第 6 部分，带插图）🌴🐱‍💻 | How web browsers work - creating the accessibility tree (part 6, with illustrations)🌴🐱‍💻
item.description:
除了到目前为止我们一直在讨论的所有这些树（DOM、CSSOM 和 AST）之外，浏览器还构建了一种称为accessibility tree的东西。

Accessibility (often abbreviated to A11y — as in, "a", then 11 characters, and then "y") in web development means enabling as many people as possible to use websites, even when those people's abilities are limited in some way. For many people, technology makes things easier. For people with disabilities, technology makes things possible. Accessibility means developing content to be as accessible as possible, no matter an individual's physical and cognitive abilities and how they access the web (ACT).

一般来说，残疾用户可以并且确实使用具有各种辅助技术的网页。 他们使用屏幕阅读器、放大镜、眼动追踪、语音命令等。 为了使这些技术中的任何一个起作用，它们需要能够访问页面的内容。 而且由于他们不能直接读取 DOM，因此 ACT 开始发挥作用。

可访问性树是使用 DOM 构建的，稍后辅助设备将使用它来解析和解释我们正在访问的网页的内容。 每个需要暴露给辅助技术的 DOM 元素都将在 ACT 中有一个对应节点。 在未构建 ACT 之前，屏幕阅读器无法访问内容。

要查看可访问性树的实际外观，您可以通过转到您选择的页面来使用 Google Chrome。 打开调试器 (F12) 并转到Elements选项卡。 从那里，您可以在右侧选择“ Accessibility ”窗格。

我去了谷歌并检查了搜索输入，这是我在Computed下的Accessibility pane中得到的：

使用语义 HTML 的重要性超出了本文的范围，但作为开发人员，我们都应该记住，我们构建的网站应该提供给所有希望使用它们的人。 如果您想阅读有关该主题的更多信息，可以在此处找到一篇关于 Web 可访问性的很好的介绍性文章。 根据互联网协会可访问性特别兴趣小组的数据，目前全世界有超过 13 亿人（约占世界人口的 15%）患有某种形式的残疾。

资源参考：



原文:

Besides all these trees we've been talking about until now (DOM, CSSOM and AST), browsers also build something called the accessibility tree.

Accessibility (often abbreviated to A11y — as in, "a", then 11 characters, and then "y") in web development means enabling as many people as possible to use websites, even when those people's abilities are limited in some way. For many people, technology makes things easier. For people with disabilities, technology makes things possible. Accessibility means developing content to be as accessible as possible, no matter an individual's physical and cognitive abilities and how they access the web (ACT).

In general, disabled users can and do use web pages with a variety of assistive technologies. They use screenreaders, magnifiers, eye tracking, voice commands and more. In order for any of these technologies to work, they need to be able to access the page's content. And since they can't read the DOM directly, the ACT comes into play.

The accessibility tree is built using the DOM and it will be later used by the assistive devices to parse and interpret the content of the webpage we are visiting. ACT is like a semantic version of the DOM and it gets updated every time the DOM gets updated. Each DOM element that needs to be exposed to assistive technologies will have a corespondent node in the ACT. Until the ACT is not built, the content is not accessible to screen readers.

To view what the accessibility tree actually looks like, you can use Google Chrome by going to a page of your choosing. Open the debugger (F12) and go to the Elements tab. From there, on the right side you can select the Accessibility pane.

I went to Google and inspected the search input and this is what I got in the Accessibility pane under Computed:

The importance of using semantic HTML is beyond the scope of this article, but as developers, we should all keep in mind that the websites we build should be made availabe the everyone who wishes to use them. If you want to read more on the subject, a good introductory article into web accessibility can be found here. According to The Internet Society Accessibility Special Interest Group, there are now over 1.3 billion people worldwide – about 15 percent of the world's population – that experience some form of disability.

item.description:
任何房间都可以通过该特定房间的 URL 或房间 ID 随时访问，并且始终可以获取和修改最后更新的代码。

如何在本地运行

如果您愿意，请做 ⭐ 回购，并随时通过提高 PR 做出贡献



原文:

➤ Jaydip Dey

➤ Archan Banerjee

## Old Features:
✅ Users can create their own room as well as share link of their rooms.

✅ All the users in a particular room can update the same code simultaneously at the same time.

✅ If anyone of the room member runs the code, the input and the output will be visible to all the members of that room.

✅ The code snippets on changing the language are updated in real time. Any room can be accessed anytime with the URL or the room ID of that particular room, and can always get and modify the last updated code.

✅ Supports 7 programming languages including C, C++, Java, Python etc.

✅ Users can select themes (github, monokai etc.) according to their choices .

✅ They can also choose the font size.

## New Features:

✅ Users can convert image to code.

✅ Users can upload any code.

✅ Users can download the code they've written.

How to run locally

➡️ Clone the repository

➡️ Create a config.env file and add MONGO_URI and PORT with your own credentials.

➡️Run npm install both inside the client folder and the root folder.

➡️Run node server in the root folder and npm start in the client folder.

➡️App starts running on port 3000. You are good to code!

*Screenshots: *

Do ⭐ the repo if you like and feel free to contribute by raising PR

item.title: 我创建了一个连接 Rails 和 Digdag 的小型库 | I've created a tiny library connecting Rails and Digdag
item.description:
藤井肯特

发表于我创建了一个连接 Rails 和 Digdag 的小型库。
图书馆“ mogura ”现在可用。

这是干什么用的？

但是，批处理往往会使代码库膨胀并增加运营成本。

在某些情况下，引入 Airflow 等专业的 Python ETL 工具可能会成为学习/构建/运营/费用或招聘人力资源的障碍。 此外，将 Rake 任务中包含的 Rails 相关领域逻辑重写为 Python 中的微服务通常不是一个现实的选择。

可能需要在 Rails 应用程序中包含工作流引擎。

我一直在寻找一种方法来使用 Rails 应用程序的域逻辑，同时仍然满足工作流引擎的要求。
我想出了创建一个连接 Rails 和 Digdag 的小型库的选项。

用法

将以下内容添加到 Rails 项目 Gemfile 和 `bundle install

ruby gem 'mogura' 安装库后，在 Rails 项目的根目录中尝试以下命令

命令bundle exec mogura push会将sample.dig注册到 Digdag。
通过在sample.dig中从 Digdag 加载 Rails 环境，您可以从工作流中调用 Rails 的域逻辑。

谢谢阅读！

目前只能将单个 Digdag 项目注册为工作流，但我认为如果可以注册多个 Digdag 项目会更好。 此外，这个库目前只允许从 CLI 进行 Digdag 操作，但我希望能够从 Rails 应用程序的域逻辑中注册和编辑工作流。

我正在积极寻找对该库的反馈或贡献，请随时添加新的功能想法！ 尼古拉斯莳萝 - Nov 1 '21 21 年 11 月 1 日

Alef Ojeda de Oliveira - Alef Ojeda de Oliveira - Oct 31 '21 21 年 10 月 31 日

poudyal_rabin - poudyal_rabin - Oct 26 '21 21 年 10 月 26 日

Jason Leow ~ golifelog.com - Jason Leow ~ golifelog.com - Oct 31 '21 21 年 10 月 31 日



原文:

Kent Fujii

Posted on May 8

I've created a tiny library connecting Rails and Digdag.
The library "mogura" is now available.

What is this used for?

Rake tasks are a way to manage batches in Rails.
However, batch processing tends to bloat the codebase and increase operational costs.

In some cases, introducing a professional Python ETL tool such as Airflow may be a hurdle with learning/building/operating/expenses or recruiting human resources. In addition, it is often not a realistic option to rewrite the Rails-related domain logic contained in Rake tasks as microservices in Python.

There may be a demand to include a workflow engine within a Rails application.

I have been looking for a way to use the domain logic of a Rails application while still satisfying the requirements of a workflow engine.
And I have come up with the option of creating a tiny library connecting Rails and Digdag.

Usage

Add the following to your Rails project Gemfile and `bundle install

ruby gem 'mogura'`

Once the library is installed, try the following command in the root directory of your Rails project

$ bundle exec mogura help Commands: mogura help [COMMAND] # Describe available commands or one specific command mogura init # Initialize Digdag files mogura push # Push Digdag workflows mogura version # Prints version

`

bundle exec mogura init will create the following files in your Rails project

`

RAILS_ROOT/config/digdag/sample.dig
RAILS_ROOT/app/dags/sample_dag.rb


`

`

_export:
  rb:
    require: {{ RAILS_ROOT }}/config/environment
+run:
  rb>: SampleDag.run


`sample_dag.rb

`

class SampleDag
  def run
    puts "Hello Rails #{Rails.env}"
  end
end

Commanding bundle exec mogura push will register sample.dig with Digdag.
By loading the Rails environment from Digdag inside sample.dig, you can call your domain logic of the Rails from your workflow.

Thanks for reading!

Currently, only a single Digdag project can be registered as a workflow, but I think it would be better if multiple Digdag projects could be registered. Also, this library currently allows Digdag operations only from the CLI, but I would like to be able to register and edit workflows from your domain logic of the Rails application.

And I'm actively looking for feedback or contributions to this library, feel free to have new functionality ideas to add! :)

Nicholas Dill - Nov 1 '21

Alef Ojeda de Oliveira - Oct 31 '21

poudyal_rabin - Oct 26 '21

Jason Leow ~ golifelog.com - Oct 31 '21
item.author: kentfujii

item.author: ejmalsi

item.title: Javascript 中的可选链接 | Optional chaining in Javascript
item.description:
可选链接是一种安全访问嵌套属性的方法，而不会出现讨厌的 Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property "something" of undefined。

假设我们有一个对象，例如

const user = { name: "Chris", details: { age: 30, email: "chris@xyz.com", } }
 const userAge = user.details.age; // 30 const userStreet = user.details.address.street; // Error !!!

访问不存在的属性的任何属性将导致错误。 我们可以通过进行多项检查来轻松解决此错误，例如

const userAddress = user.details.address ? user.details.address.street : null;

如果有更多嵌套属性或者顶级属性不存在，这只会变得更丑陋。 我曾经使用 lodash _.get 来解决这个问题。 但是 javascript 现在已经为我们提供了可选链接来克服这个问题，而无需使用任何额外的依赖项。 它就像这样：

const userAddress = user.details.address?.street

可选链接?. 如果?. 是未定义或 null 并返回未定义。 这比我们以前的干净得多。 还要记住?. 只是将值保留在它之前而不是之后。 之后的属性会定期访问。

可选链接也适用于方括号和函数，如下所示：

obj?.[prop] -> returns obj[prop] if obj exists, otherwise undefined. obj?.[prop] -> 如果 obj 存在则返回 obj[prop]，否则未定义。
obj.method?.() -> calls obj.method() if obj.method exists, otherwise returns undefined. obj.method?.() -> 如果 obj.method 存在则调用 obj.method()，否则返回 undefined。



原文:

Optional chaining is a way to safely access nested properties without getting the nasty Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property "something" of undefined.

Suppose, we have an object such as

const user = {
    name: "Chris",
    details: {
        age: 30,
        email: "chris@xyz.com",
    }
}

const userAge = user.details.age; // 30
const userStreet = user.details.address.street; // Error !!!

Accessing any property of a non-existent property will result in an error. We can easily resolve this error by having multiple checks like

const userAddress = user.details.address ? user.details.address.street : null;

This just gets uglier if there are more nested properties or if the top-level properties don't exist. I used to use lodash _.get to resolve this. But javascript has now given us optional chaining to overcome this without using any extra dependency. It just goes like this:

const userAddress = user.details.address?.street

The optional chaining ?. stops the evaluation if the value before ?. is undefined or null and returns undefined. This is much cleaner than what we had before. Also bear in mind that ?. just keeps the value optional before it and not after that. The properties after that are accessed regularly.

The optional chaining also works with square brackets and functions like so:

obj?.[prop] -> returns obj[prop] if obj exists, otherwise undefined.
obj.method?.() -> calls obj.method() if obj.method exists, otherwise returns undefined.
item.author: anjalyyy1

item.title: 为什么我们在 Gatsby + Strapi 而不是 WordPress 中建立了我们的主网站。 | Why we have built our main website in Gatsby + Strapi instead of WordPress.
item.description:
迪玛格罗斯曼

发表于今天，WordPress 是世界上最可靠的 CRM 之一。 我敢说这是让 PHP 保持活力的原因之一。

并且有充分的理由，借助 Elementor 和 Divi 等网站建设者，产品营销人员的工作可以在拖放环境中访问。

那么为什么是盖茨比？

我们可以说它主要是为了 SSG 和速度，但事实并非如此。
您可以拥有令人惊叹的预渲染插件来实现几乎相同的结果。

我们正在https://github.com/novuhq/novu构建第一个开源通知基础设施。 我们的产品是由社区打造的，没有我们的社区支持，就没有真正的产品。

这意味着它应该完全可供社区分叉、贡献和使用。

这就是为什么在 Novu，我们将所有内容都构建为公开的，您可以在此处找到我们的网站和后端源代码：
但深入挖掘，你会发现一些其他重要的注意事项，例如：

  • 您不会发现同时具有阶段和生产环境的网站可以轻松实现。
  • 我们的主要应用程序 UI 是使用 React 构建的。 因此，贡献者无需学习新的框架或语言就可以更容易地深入代码库。
  • 我们可以运行异步代码，而无需使用 hacky ubuntu cron 作业。
  • 我们可以直接在我们的网站上使用我们的 Novu 库。
  • 我们可以使用 GraphQL 轻松查询我们的数据库数据。 例如，您是否尝试过查询 wp_posts？

您认为我们做出了正确的选择吗？ 无论如何，我们应该使用 WordPress 吗？ 我们应该使用 Next.js 还是 Remix？

让我知道你的想法。



原文:

Dima Grossman

Posted on May 8 • Originally published at novu.co

Today WordPress is one of the most reliable CRMs in the world. I dare to say that this is one of the things that keeps PHP alive.

And for a good reason, with website builders such as Elementor and Divi, Product Marketer jobs become accessible in a drag and drop environment.

So why Gatsby?

We can say that it's mainly for SSG and speed, but that's not the case.
You can have amazing pre-render plugins to achieve almost the same results.

We are building the 1st open-source notifications infrastructure at https://github.com/novuhq/novu . Our product is built by the community, and there is no really a product without our community support.

It means it should be fully available for the community to fork, contribute and use.

This is why at Novu, we build everything public, you can find our website and backend source here:
https://github.com/novuhq/website
https://github.com/novuhq/cms

But digging deeper, you can find some other important notes, for example:

  • WordPress suffers from migrations. You won't find websites with both stage and production environments easily implemented.
  • Our main application UI is built with React. So it's a lot easier for contributors to dive into the codebase without learning a new framework or language.
  • CI/CD is a lot easier, just a few clicks away with Gatsby.
  • We can run asynchronous code without using a hacky ubuntu cron jobs.
  • We can use our Novu libraries directly with our website.
  • We can query our database data easily with GraphQL. For example, have you tried to query wp_posts?

Do you think we have made the right choice? Should we have gone with WordPress anyway? Should we have gone with Next.js or Remix?

Let me know what you think.
item.author: scopsy

item.title: Linux 安装问题 | Linux Installation Problems
item.description:
藤虎25

发表于我无法让 Linux 在旧台式计算机上运行。 这是一台 HP Compaq DC5800 微型 PC，已被修复（不是我修复的，而是很久以前修复的）。 它由 Intel Core 2 Duo E4600 处理器提供支持，这是一个 64 位处理器，但目前可用的 Windows 版本（Vista 和 7）是 32 位的。

因为我最喜欢基于 Debian 的发行版，所以我尝试了 x86 和 x86 64 版本的 Debian 和 Ubuntu。 我唯一的成功是使用 32 位 Ubuntu 17.04，但是一旦我更新它（不是发行版升级，只是普通更新），它就开始无法启动。



原文:

fujitora25

Posted on May 8

I'm having trouble getting Linux to work on an old desktop computer. It's an HP Compaq DC5800 tiny form factor PC that's been restored (not by me, but a long time ago). It's powered by an Intel Core 2 Duo E4600 processor, which is a 64-bit processor, however the Windows versions that have worked thus far (Vista and 7) are 32-bit.

Because I'm most comfortable with Debian-based distributions, I tried both x86 and x86 64 versions of Debian and Ubuntu on it. The only success I had was with 32-bit Ubuntu 17.04, however it started failing to boot as soon as I updated it (not a distro upgrade, just ordinary updates).

原文:

I just stumbled upon this Tweet from Elon Musk on my timeline and I feel the need to share it. I usually avoid dealing with polarizing topics like this because there are just too many different opinions, but this is just a very important issue to me.


This topic resonates with me so much, and I think it's important coming from one of the most influential people on the planet.

In my opinion, companies, software and non-software alike, should move towards valuing and promoting technical excellence at all levels of the hierarchy. This is not necessarily about programming, but about having deep technical knowledge in the area you manage.

What you do you think?

item.title: 端到端 GraphQL 错误处理？ | End-to-end GraphQL error handling?
item.description:
我想分享我在 GraphQL 解析器中处理错误的方法（我在服务器端使用GraphQL Yoga ）并将它们传递到前端（我使用Svelte + @urql/svelte ）。

后端

包装错误信息

我发现，从 GraphQL 端点返回错误的唯一方法是在解析器中抛出错误。 该设计对在后端和前端之间传输错误数据施加了一些限制。 让我们想象一些错误的请求来了，服务器无法处理，并且您想返回一个错误，并且可能返回一些关于究竟出了什么问题的详细信息。 假设我们要将所有信息打包到如下界面中：

 type ApiError = { code : 400 , message : " The request was bad :( " }

回到限制 - 没有办法将类似对象的东西作为参数放入new Error(arg)调用中，但我们可以将ApiError对象转换为 JSON 字符串。 这是一个奇怪的小技巧，它允许将任何信息打包到一个 throwable 中。 这就是信息传输的全部内容，但还有一些关于代码组织的事情。
通过抛出错误从 GraphQL 端点返回错误可能很方便，并且使用 Typescript 断言函数是类型安全的。 无需将 return 语句包装到if语句中； 只是抛出一个错误并保持一个扁平的代码结构。 让我们将其与打包信息结合到一个错误对象中：

 export const newApiError = ( errorObject : ApiError ) => { return new Error ( JSON . stringify ( errorObject )) }

现在可以throw newApiError(apiError) 。

但是仍然需要检查我们是否需要抛出，这意味着if语句。 我们能做得更好吗？ 是的，使用打字稿断言功能。

摆脱if语句

假设后端在端点上接收请求需要授权，但请求中没有凭据。 看起来我们想说“401：未授权，没有信用，你不能通过”。 那么，在解析器中最简单的方法是什么？ 我认为以下内容：

 // get an auth token somehow const authToken string | null = req . headers . get ( " Authorization " ) assert ( authToken , " 401: There are no creds, you shall not pass " )

自定义断言

但这是唯一的字符串； 因为我们不允许将任何像对象这样的东西传递到断言调用中，所以让我们编写断言的实现：

 export function assertWithApiError < T > ( statement : T | null | undefined , errorObject : ApiError ): asserts statement is T { if ( ! statement ) { throw newApiError ( errorObject ) } }

前端

前端部分呢？ 正如我上面所说，我在我的 svelte 应用程序中使用 @svelte/urql 包来处理 GraphQL。 让我们看一下代码！ 从常见的@svelte/urql 用例开始：

 // mutation function is from @svelte/urql package const editUserMutation = mutation < { me : User } > ({ query : EDIT_USER , }) const { data , error } = await editMeMutation ({ intro })

所以，@svelte/urql 的错误是CombinedError类型，其中包括 GraphQL 原始错误的数组：

 type OriginalError = { message : string stack : string } export const parseGraphqlApiErrors = ( error : CombinedError ): ApiError [] => error . graphQLErrors . map (( e ) => { const rawOriginalError = ( e . extensions ?. originalError as OriginalError ). message return parseApiError ( rawOriginalError ) }) export const parseApiError = ( jsonString : string ): ApiError => { try { const parsed : unknown = JSON . parse ( jsonString ) if ( apiErrorTypeGuard ( parsed )) { return parsed } else { throw new Error ( ' got invalid api error ' ) } } catch ( e ) { console . error ( e ) throw Error ( " Can't parse api error from json string " ) } } const apiErrorTypeGuard = ( possiblyError : any ): possiblyError is ApiError => typeof possiblyError === ' object ' && ' code ' in possiblyError && ' message ' in possiblyError

在故事的最后，我们有自定义断言，这些断言会抛出错误，里面塞满了我们想要的任何数据，以及可以提取该数据的前端代码； 就是这样。

我不是 GraphQL、Svelte 或 Urql 的经验丰富的用户，如果您向我指出任何比上述更好的现有解决方案，我会很高兴，并希望我的想法对某人有用:)

马里奥·门德斯摄



原文:

I want to share my approach to handling errors in GraphQL resolvers (I use GraphQL Yoga on the server-side) and pass them to the frontend side (where I use Svelte + @urql/svelte).

Backend

Packing error info

As I figured out, the only way to return an error from a GraphQL endpoint is by throwing an error in a resolver. That design imposes some restrictions on transferring error data between the backend and frontend. Let's imagine some bad request came that the server can't process, and you want to return an error and probably, some details about what exactly went wrong. Suppose that we want to pack all info in the following interface:

type ApiError = { code: 400, message: "The request was bad :("
}

Back to restrictions - there is no way to put something object-like as an argument into the new Error(arg) call, but we can transform the ApiError object into a JSON string. That is the weird little trick that allows packing any information into a throwable. That's all about information transfer, but there are a couple more things about code organization.
Return error from GraphQL endpoint by throwing error could be convenient and a type safe with Typescript assert functions. There is no need to wrap return statements into if statements; just throw an error and keep a flat code structure. Let's combine that with packing information into an error object:

export const newApiError = (errorObject: ApiError) => { return new Error(JSON.stringify(errorObject))
}

Now it's possible to do throw newApiError(apiError).

But it's still necessary to check whether we need to throw, which means if statements. Could we do better? Yes, with typescript assert functions.

Getting rid of if statements

Imagine that a backend receiving a request on an endpoint requires authorization, but there are no creds in the request. It looks like we want to say, "401: Not authorized, there are no creds, you shall not pass." So, what is the easiest way to do it in resolver? I suppose that the following:

// get an auth token somehow
const authToken string | null = req.headers.get("Authorization") assert( authToken, "401: There are no creds, you shall not pass"
)

Custom assert

But it's the only string; as we are not allowed to pass anything like an object into an assert call, so let's write our realization of assert:

export function assertWithApiError<T>( statement: T | null | undefined, errorObject: ApiError
): asserts statement is T { if (!statement) { throw newApiError(errorObject) }
}

Typescript assert function allows us to have typesafe assertions in which we can throw any throwable even created by ourselves, with any data packed into error.

// get an auth token somehow
const authToken: string | null = req.headers.get("Authorization")
assertWithApiError( authToken, { message: 'Auth token is not presented', code: 401 }
)
// there is typescript knows that token is a string

Frontend

What about a frontend part? As I said above, I use @svelte/urql package for dealing with GraphQL in my svelte app. Let's look at the code! Start with the common @svelte/urql use case:

// mutation function is from @svelte/urql package
const editUserMutation = mutation<{ me: User }>({ query: EDIT_USER,
}) const { data, error } = await editMeMutation({ intro })

So, the error there is CombinedError type from @svelte/urql, which includes an array of GraphQL original errors:


type OriginalError = { message: string stack: string
} export const parseGraphqlApiErrors = (error: CombinedError): ApiError[] => error.graphQLErrors.map((e) => { const rawOriginalError = (e.extensions?.originalError as OriginalError).message return parseApiError(rawOriginalError)
}) export const parseApiError = (jsonString: string): ApiError => { try { const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(jsonString) if (apiErrorTypeGuard(parsed)) { return parsed } else { throw new Error('got invalid api error') } } catch (e) { console.error(e) throw Error("Can't parse api error from json string") }
} const apiErrorTypeGuard = (possiblyError: any): possiblyError is ApiError => typeof possiblyError === 'object' && 'code' in possiblyError && 'message' in possiblyError

At the end of the story, we have custom assertions that are throwing errors stuffed with any data we want and frontend code that can extract that data; that's it.

I'm not a very experienced user of GraphQL, Svelte, or Urql, and I would be happy if you point me to any existing solution which is better than that described above and hope my ideas will come in handy for someone :)

Photo by Mario Mendez

item.title: 用于 DevOps 的 YAML。 | YAML for DevOps.
item.description:
安诺卡尔古斯塔

发表于它已成为一种广泛使用的格式，用于为许多不同的 DevOps 工具和应用程序编写配置，例如 Docker、Kubernetes、Ansible、Prometheus。

因此，在使用此工具时，了解 YAML 语言结构及其主要概念的微妙之处非常重要。

就像 XML 和 JSON 一样。

什么是序列化语言？

使用序列化语言，用不同语言和不同技术编写的应用程序可以使用共同商定的或标准格式交换数据。 最标准的格式是 YAML、JSON 和 XML。

可以使用扩展名创建 YAML 文件：.yaml

他们都是一样的。

因为它具有超强的可读性和直观性，所以非常适合为所有最近的 DevOps 工具（如 Docker、Kubernetes 等）编写配置文件。

我们将通过这个示例向您展示 YAML、XML 和 JSON 格式的比较。 以下是 YAML 的外观：

这很简单，而且非常干净。

以下是 XML 中的相同数据：

这是 JSON 格式的相同代码：

任何有效的 JSON 文件都可以用 YAML 编码。

如您所见，XML 和 JSON 数据结构是使用特殊字符定义的。

在 XML 中，文本位于尖括号 <> 中，而在 JSON 中，文本位于大括号 {} 中。 在 YAML 中，您没有这些特殊字符。

它是通过行分隔和带有缩进的空格来定义的。

这就是为什么您可以在 JSON 和 XML 中使用空格和缩进，但不能在 YAML 中使用。

再见。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— May 3 5月3日



原文:

Annoh Karlgusta

Posted on May 8

YAML has become very popular for writing configuration files. It has become a widely used format for writing configurations for many different DevOps tools and applications e.g. Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Prometheus.

For that reason while utilizing this instruments, understanding the subtleties of YAML language structure and its primary concepts is significant.

YAML is a data serialization language. Just like XML and JSON.

What is a Serialization language?

With a serialization language, applications written in different languages and with different technologies can exchange data using a common agreed upon or standard format. The most standard formats are YAML, JSON, and XML.

YAML actually stands for YAML Ain't Markup Language.

YAML files can be created with the extension: .yaml

.yml

They are both the same.

YAML Format compared to others.

YAML has grown in popularity over the past years due to:
Because it is super human readable and intuitive, it is perfect for writing configuration files for all those recent DevOps tools like Docker, Kubernetes, etc.

We'll consider this example to show you how YAML, XML, and JSON formats compare. Here's how YAML looks:

It's straightforward, and very clean.

Here is the same data in XML:

Here is the same code in JSON format:

The YAML encoding is a superset of the JSON encoding. Any valid JSON file can be encoded in YAML.

XML and JSON data structures are defined using special characters, as you have seen.

In XML, you have text in angle brackets <> while in JSON you have text in curly brackets {}. In YAML, you do not have those special characters.

How are data structures defined in YAML? It is defined through line separations and spaces with indentation.

That's why you can space and indent in JSON and XML, but not in YAML.

See you soon.

Cristian Fernando - May 3

"Don't ever loose hope in yourself. Be persistent what you are doing." “永远不要对自己失去希望。坚持你正在做的事情。”

item.title: 工程管理技巧#3 | Engineering Management Tip #3
item.description:
我认为经理应该关注三个原则或长期优先事项。 一方面，我相信，就像朱莉周在《经理人的培养》中提到的那样，经理的工作是掌握团队的成果。 虽然她继续将其与安迪·格鲁夫（Andy Grove）对教育团队成员（因此他们拥有技能）并激励他们并调整愿景（因此他们有动力从事对团队至关重要），但这些对我来说并不是困难的部分。 困难的部分是愿景的灵感和一致性，80% 的价值来自于此——团队中不成比例的影响。 你是怎么做到的？

同样，我认为您必须从上到下遵循三个原则。 接受培训以取悦客户并为您的团队实现超大成果。 但仍然冷静地专注于这一点：

  • 快乐（或重新设置/找到重新快乐的方法以便愿意提供帮助）
  • 有同理心（关于挣扎的人，不仅仅是“容易”）
  • 激发兴奋（关于应对技术挑战）

这三个方面对于对您的团队和组织产生巨大影响至关重要。

要开心

第一个，快乐，真的很重要。 您是否需要了解更多有关 3D 的知识来激发您的兴趣并从中获得乐趣？ 周末断线需要充电吗？ 具有讽刺意味的是，最大限度地提高个人幸福感非常重要，因为从周一到周五的每一次互动都涉及到这种幸福感。 你不会再往前走，推动自己，向上、向下、横向、向内、向外——无论你看到什么维度——你在与他人的所有互动中的效率都与你自己的幸福感高度相关。

你有治疗师吗？ 嗯，你应该。 如果你是一个天生的怪胎，并且你的直系亲属或朋友圈中发生的事情或你对世界新闻的看法没有问题，那么请务必与治疗师交谈并继续培养这种令人惊叹的生活方式，以便您可以将其传递给其他人（老实说，写下来！）。 如果你更正常，那么治疗师和/或教练会帮助你疏通一些需要更多成长才能更有效的事情。 问题很简单：你是世界上最幸福的人吗？ 每次你进入房间时，你有感染力的幸福感是否会像某种欢快的音乐剧一样感染人们？ 如果没有，治疗师可以帮助您在这方面做得更好。 它在工作之外也非常有用。 所有这些最终都会影响你处理工作的方式。 要开心。 即使生活面临严峻挑战，也要找到快乐的方法。 尽管经历了重大的生活挑战，你能成长吗，一次三个？ 你需要认真的韧性和快乐。 你管理的越高，这需要越强大。

有同理心

在你的团队中有一个有挑战的人是很正常的。 帮助他们一点是很正常的。 在某些时候停止是正常的。 和图 - 哦，这对他们有好处。 让他们多尝试一点。

有时这种方法有效。 如何让它变得更好？ 留住你们俩都在处理更长一点的模棱两可的问题。 多想想他们拥有哪些技能，哪些经验和信心，以及这对他们来说是否是一项挑战。

有时，同事会显得不那么投入。 您可能会正确或错误地理解这一点。 他们发布了一个愚蠢的模因，当然他们有更多任务的带宽。

但是，如果他们只是因为一个家庭成员正在经历可怕的事情而坚持在一起，或者他们自己也一样。 总是假设每个人都有一个他们通常不会谈论的巨大负担。 他们只是勉强把它放在一起。 总是假设因为事实是人类是复杂的，他们无疑在幕后挣扎。

作为一名经理，正如朱莉卓在她的书中提到的那样，你在向你汇报的人身上拥有更多的权力和影响力——因此至少与这种权力成正比，有同理心是很重要的。

同样，重要的方面是让自己摆脱最初对一个人的偏见，并在有同理心时“忍受模棱两可”。 根据济慈的说法，这就是人类伟大的原因。 这无疑是使领导者能够领导每个不完美的人的原因。

火花兴奋

我会说“激发创新”（这将导致您的团队取得超大成果），但这说起来容易做起来难。 创新的先决条件是兴奋。 这也是让每个人最终都能在实际创新上取得成功的原因。

令人兴奋的是发现人们眼中的闪光。 从一个人真正对自己的工作感到兴奋的那一刻起倒退。 你知道电影中的节拍——它发生在故事弧线的 2/3 处，就像发条一样。

你想成为那个催化剂。 这可以是在新领域寻找和教育您的团队。 将他们与其他专家或会议联系起来，或者只是随机探索的领域，让他们更深入地研究这个主题。 这可以包括以身作则和展示最佳实践——你如何学会提高效率，你如何进行分析或权衡，你可以从你的经验中提炼出什么。 这可以从一个严重的客户问题倒退——实际上激发了团队解决这个问题的动力。

这一点非常重要，以至于一些水果公司在推出新产品或新功能时，纯粹为了在他们自己和他们的客户之间产生火花而保持秘密。 成为火花是你作为经理最有影响力的行为。

但是这些事情是有规律的。 如果你不是真的快乐，你就不会最大化你的同理心。 如果你没有深刻的同理心，你就不会激发人们真正的兴奋。 就是这么简单。

首先是花时间开心，然后是同理心，激发兴奋。

从那里开始，测量和流程开发（机制）对于管理者来说非常重要。 但这是另一个提示。



原文:

There's three principles or long-term priorities I think a manager should focus on. While on the one hand I believe, like Julie Zhou mentions in The Making of a Manager, that a manager's job is to own the outcomes of the team. To "get better outcomes from a team of people working together." And while she continues to break it down (super helpfully I might add) by connecting this to Andy Grove's focus on educating team members (so they have the skills) and inspiring them and aligning the vision (so they have the motivation to work on what's crucial for the team), yet those are to me not the hard parts. The hard part is inspiration and alignment on the vision, where the 80% of value comes from - the out-of-proportion impact in the group. How do you get to that?

Again I think there are three principles that you have to work top to bottom from. Trained towards delighting customers and achieving oversized outcomes for your team. But nonetheless calmly focused on this:

  • Be happy (or reset/find ways to be happy again so as to be willing to help)
  • Have empathy (about people struggling, more than is “easy”)
  • Spark excitement (about tackling technical challenges)

These three aspects are critical to having oversized impact on your team and the organization.

Be happy

The first one, being happy, is really important. Do you need to learn more about 3D to pique your interest and have fun? Do you need to recharge on the weekend disconnecting? It is ironically extremely important that you maximize your personal happiness because every interaction you have from Monday through Friday touches on this level of happiness. You won't go the next mile and push yourself, managing upwards, downwards, sideways, inside, outside - whatever dimension you look at - your effectiveness in all interactions with other people is highly correlated with your own happiness.

Do you have a therapist? Well, you should. If you're a freak of nature and you have no problems going on in your immediate family or circle of friends or how you perceive the world news, then by all means talk to a therapist and continue to cultivate this amazing approach to life so that you can pass it on to others (honestly, write about it!). And if you're more normal, then a therapist and/or coach will help unblock some of the things that need more growth for you to be more effective. The question is simple: are you the happiest person in the world? Does your infectious happiness rub off people like some sort of up-beat musical every time you enter the room? If not, a therapist can help you get a little better in this area. It's also super useful for outside of work too. All of which ultimately affect how you approach your work. Be happy. Find robust ways to be happy even when life confronts you with serious challenges. Can you grow despite major life challenges through at you, three at a time? You need serious resilience with happiness. And the higher up you manage, the stronger this needs to be.

Have empathy

It is very normal to have someone on your team who is having a challenge. And it’s normal to help them out a bit. And it’s normal at some point to maybe stop. And figure - oh it’s, good for them. Let them try a little more.

Sometimes this approach works. How to make it better? Dwell with the ambiguity that you’re both tackling a little longer. Think a little more about which skills they have and which experience and confidence and whether this is good for them as a challenge.

Sometimes a colleague will seem less engaged. You’ll perhaps perceive this correctly or incorrectly. They posted a silly meme, surely they have bandwidth for more tasks.

But what if they’re just holding it together because a family member is going through something horrible, or they themselves are as well. Always assume everyone has a great burden that they’re carrying that they don’t normally talk about. And that they’re just barely holding it together. Always assume that because the truth is being human is complicated and they are undoubtedly struggling with something behind the scenes.

As a manager, again as Julie Zhuo mentions in her book, you have more power and influence in those who report to you - so at least proportional to this amount of power it is important to be empathetic.

Again, the important aspect is to push yourself past your initial prejudices about a person and “dwell with the ambiguity” in having empathy. This is, according to Keats, what makes a human great. It is certainly what makes a leader capable of leading everyone who is not perfect.

Spark excitement

I would say “spark innovation” (that will lead to your team’s oversized outcomes) but that’s easier said than done. A prerequisite of innovation is excitement. It’s also what keeps everyone going to finally turn the corner on actual innovation.

Sparking excitement is finding the glint in people’s eye. Working backwards from the moment where a person becomes genuinely excited about their work. You know the beat in movies - it happens about 2/3rds of the way through a story arc like clockwork.

You want to be a catalyst for that. This can be finding and educating your team on new areas. Connecting them with other experts or conferences or just random areas to explore and for them to go deeper in the subject. This can include leading by example and showing best practices - how you’ve learned to be more efficient, how you approach analysis or trade offs, what you can distill from your experiences. This can be working backwards from a serious customer problem - and actually sparking the team’s momentum towards addressing it.

This aspect is so critical that some fruit companies remain secretive purely for the spark among themselves and their customers when they debut new products or features. Being the spark is your most impactful act as a manager.

But there is an order to these things. You will not maximize your empathy if you are not truly happy. And if you don’t have deep empathy you won’t spark real excitement in people. It’s that simple.

It starts with taking time to be happy, then having empathy, and sparking excitement.

From there measuring and process development (mechanisms) are hugely important as a manager. But that’s for another tip.
item.author: p10q

item.title: 云的类型 | Type of Clouds
item.description:
在上一篇博客中，我们了解了云的介绍。 今天，我们将了解云的类型。

请记住：您使用哪个云提供商并不重要。 云的类型将保持不变。

我们有 4 种不同类型的云：

公共云是一个共享的地方，硬件和资源之间没有物理分离。 任何使用公共云部署其应用程序或使用任何资源的人都将在同一个共享位置。 但是，他们将无法访问彼此的应用程序或资源。

通俗地说，公共云是一个很大的空间，云提供商会根据客户的要求分配空间或资源或计算资源。 公共云（硬件）没有物理隔离。

例如：把它想象成一个公共网球场，每个人都来打球，但他们都在自己的球场上。

安全性是公共云中的一个问题。 在云中，“云的安全”是云提供商的责任，而“云中的安全”是所有者的责任。

好处：

因为它是共享资源和基础设施。 企业将只为使用付费。

在设计和实施解决方案时，开发人员和组织可以专注于实际工作，而不必担心底层复杂性

缺点：

安全可能是一个问题。

在哪里使用它：

  • 测试环境

  • 静态应用程序或软件开发。

  • 要求很高且可预测的地方。

  • 专注于业务运营的应用程序

例子：

例如：我的a11ytips.dev托管在公共云上。 我正在使用 S3 来托管它。

当数据安全是优先事项时，私有云非常有用。 私有云不是共享空间，而是由单个客户专用。

  • 硬件是物理隔离的，不被其他任何人共享。 它仅适用于一位客户。

    • 例如：把它想象成在你的地方建立你自己的私人网球场，只有授权的人才能进入。

    好处：

    • 安全性：由于硬件是隔离的并且访问受限。 安全是最好的。

    • 完全控制：一个人将对环境有 100% 的控制权，并且不依赖于云提供商。 作为一个可以添加自己的资源。

    • 性能：因为解决方案将专用于业务。 性能问题不会存在。

    • 灵活性：拥有环境和资源的好处是拥有基于需求或不断变化的需求的灵活环境。

    权衡：

    • 时间：在私有云中，一个人欠基础设施和资源。 与公共云相比，他们需要花费大量时间从头开始做所有事情、管理和按需扩展它。

    • 管理：需要管理整个解决方案，需要时间和金钱

    • 成本：由于这是一个具有硬件和资源的专用解决方案，与公共云相比，价格会更高。

    • 可扩展性：与公共云相比，基础设施并不像按需扩展那么容易。

    在哪里使用它：

    • 数据安全至关重要的任何地方，例如 - 政府或任何研发组织

    • 任何具有安全性或大型或高数据中心要求的组织。

    例子：

    处理货币和用户敏感信息的银行应用程序应该使用私有云。

    这是公有云和私有云的结合。 可以将资源组合在一起以构建解决方案。

  • 客户可以合并私有云和公共云功能和资源，以充分利用两者。 对于敏感信息，将使用私有云，对于非敏感信息，将使用公共云。

  • 这些资源通常被编排为一个集成的基础设施环境。

    • 现在，您将为两者付费并管理它们之间的时间。 好处：



    原文:

    In the last blog, we read about the introduction of the cloud. Today, we will learn about types of cloud.

    Remember: It Doesn’t matter which cloud provider you use. Types of clouds would remain same.

    We have 4 different types of clouds:

    1) Public

    • A public cloud is a shared place with no physical separation between the hardware & resources. Anyone who is using the public cloud to deploy their application or using any resources will be at the same shared place. However, they won’t have access to each other apps, or resources.

    In a layman language, the public cloud is a big space and the cloud provider will assign the space or resources or computing resources as required by customer. Public cloud (hardware) are not physically segregated.

    Eg: think it like a public tennis court where everyone is coming and playing but they are in their court.

    Security is a concern in public cloud. In the cloud, the ‘security of the cloud’ is the responsibility of the cloud provider but ‘security in the cloud’ is the owner’s responsibility.

    Benefits:

    1) Cost-effective: Public cloud is cost-effective. As it is a shared resource and infra. The business will be paying only for the use.

    2) Scalable: The solution can be easily scalable on-demand.

    3) Flexible: The cloud solution on the public cloud is flexible as there are ample resources

    4) Reduce complexity: A lot of complexity is handled by cloud providers. While designing and implementing the solution developers and organisations can focus on the actual work and not worry about the underlay complexity

    5) No maintenance cost: The capital cost (CoPx) is low or almost zero.

    Drawbacks:

    1) Security: As it is a shareable cloud and multiple customers would be using it. Security could be a concern.

    2) Minimal control: As it is a public cloud then one would have minimum control for the underlying resources, hardware, network etc.

    Where to use it:

    • Test environments

    • Static application or software developments.

    • Where the demands are high and predictable.

    • Apps focused on business operations

    Examples:

    Eg: My a11ytips.dev is hosted on the public cloud. I am using S3 to host it.

    2) Private Cloud

    • The private cloud is useful when data security is a priority. A private cloud is not a shared space but will be dedicatedly used by a single customer.

    • The hardware is physically isolated and not shared by anyone else. It is solely dedicated to one customer.

    Eg: Think of it as setting up your own private tennis court at your place and accessible only by authorise person.

    Benefits:

    • Security: As the hardware is isolated and has limited access. Security is at its best.

    • Total control: One will have 100% control over the environment and doesn’t depend on the cloud provider. As one can add their own resources.

    • Performance: As the solution will be dedicated to the business. Performance issues won’t be there.

    • Flexibility: The benefit of owning the environment and resources is to have a flexible environment based on the demand or ever-changing requirements.

    Trade-offs:

    • Time: In private cloud the one owes the infra and resources. They need to spend a lot of time doing everything from scratch as compared to public cloud, manage, and on-demand scaling it.

    • Management: One needs to manage the whole solution and would require time and money

    • Cost: As this is a dedicated solution with hardware and resources the price would be higher as compared to the public cloud.

    • Scalability: Infrastructure is not as easy to scalable on-demand as compared to the public cloud.

    Where to use it:

    • Anywhere the data security is crucial such as - Government, or any R&D organisation

    • Any organisation where security or large or high data-centre requirements are.

    Example:

    A banking application dealing with the monetary and user’s sensitive information should be using a private cloud.

    3) Hybrid

    • This is the combination of Public and Private cloud. Resources can be combined together to build a solution.

    • The customer can merge both - private and public cloud features and resources to get the best out of both. For sensitive information, the private cloud will be used and for non-sensitive public cloud will be used.

    • The resources are typically orchestrated as an integrated infrastructure environment.

    _Think it like you have two tennis court one is at our place (private) and one is at community park. Now, you will be paying for both and managing your time between them. _

    Benefits:
item.author: hellonehha

item.title: 使用 Cloudflare 和您的免费自定义域的无限免费电子邮件地址 | Unlimited Free Email Address using Cloudflare and your Free Custom Domain
item.description:
我们都遇到过这个问题，我们需要提供我们的电子邮件 ID 以便在不同的网站上进行注册。 有时我们认为我们可以避免提供我们的个人或企业电子邮件地址，因为这可能会导致垃圾邮件。

现在，在互联网上，我们知道有许多网站提供免费的临时电子邮件地址。 毫无疑问，这些电子邮件地址可以完成您的工作，但它们也有一些缺点，例如它们是临时地址，因此不能长期使用，其次您需要事先创建这些地址以填写网站。 现在，由于这个技巧众所周知，许多网站会阻止来自这些网站域名的地址。

还有另一种方法可以通过添加 (+) 加号来生成无限制的 Gmail 电子邮件地址。 例如 - abc+xyz@gmail.com或abc+123@gmail.com ，这些都将转到abc@gmail.com 。 如果大多数网站没有检查带有 gmail.com 地址的加号，则此方法适用于大多数网站。

获取免费域名：转到freenom。 登录网站后，搜索可用的域名，然后选择.tk选项并结帐。 该域名免费，有效期为 1 年。 在结帐页面上，您可以将持续时间从 3 个月更改为 12 个月，然后进行结帐。 您现在将获得免费域名。

  1. 登录或创建Cloudflare帐户。 将您的域名添加到 Cloudflare 仪表板。 现在，Cloudflare 将搜索该域的现有域名记录，然后要求您修改它以指向 Cloudflare 名称服务器。 从 Cloudflare 复制名称服务器，然后转到自由、域管理和替换名称服务器。 您需要等待几分钟，直到 Cloudflare 选择新的更改。

  1. 现在，转到 Cloudflare 的电子邮件部分，它会显示一些记录丢失，您现在可以单击跳过。 单击路由选项卡，然后单击启用电子邮件路由。 最后，它会给你一个“电子邮件路由已启用并路由电子邮件”。 信息。

  1. 全部设置为创建电子邮件地址。 转到路由选项卡并滚动到目标电子邮件地址部分，将您的电子邮件地址添加到您希望接收此域的所有电子邮件的位置。 验证目标电子邮件地址后

转到“Catch-all address”部分并启用它，单击编辑并选择发送到选项，然后从下一个下拉列表中选择您已验证的电子邮件地址。 单击保存。

现在，您可以使用此域中的任何电子邮件地址。 就像您在hashnode@domainname.tk 、 devto@domainname.tk或medium@domainname.tk上收到电子邮件一样。 这些都将落在您在 cloudflare 中验证的主电子邮件地址中。

现在，在任何网站上注册时，您可以在该网站上添加任何电子邮件地址，电子邮件将登陆您的网络邮件。

这是一个包装。 让我知道您对此的反馈。 请关注更多此类技巧和教程。



原文:

We all have faced this issue where we need to provide our email id for registration purposes on different websites. Sometimes we think we can avoid giving our personal or business email addresses as this can result in spam emails.

Now, on the internet, we know there are many websites that provide free temporary email addresses. No doubt those email addresses will do your job, but there are some disadvantages to them, such as they are temporary addresses so cant be used for the long term, second you need to create those addresses beforehand to fill in the website. Now, as this trick is very well known, many sites block addresses from these websites' domain names.

There is another way to generate an unlimited Gmail email address by adding a (+) plus sign. for example - abc+xyz@gmail.com or abc+123@gmail.com , these all will go to abc@gmail.com. This method will work on most sites if they don't have a check on plus sign with gmail.com addresses.

Cloudflare Trick: We will use the free domain from the freedom website for this tutorial, if you already have a domain name registered with Cloudflare, then go to step 3.

  1. Get Free Domain Name: Go to freenom. com, and register for an account. Once login to the website, search for a domain name that is available, then select the .tk option and checkout. This domain is free of cost and valid for 1 year. On the checkout page, you can change the duration from 3 months to 12 months and then proceed for checkout. You will get your free domain name now.

  1. Login or create a Cloudflare account. Add your domain name to the Cloudflare dashboard. Now, Cloudflare will search for the existing domain name record for this domain and then ask you to modify it to point to Cloudflare name servers. Copy the name servers from Cloudflare, and go to freedom, domain management and replace the name servers. You need to wait for a few minutes till Cloudflare picks the new changes.

  1. Now, go to the email section of Cloudflare, it will show some records are missing, you can click on a skip for now. Click on the Route tab and then click on Enable Email Routing. Cloudflare will show some options you just need to accept those recommendations and apply. Finally, it will give you an "Email Routing is enabled and routing emails." message.

  1. All set to create email addresses. Go to the Routes tab and scroll to the destination email address section, add your email address where you want to receive all emails for this domain. Once you verify the destination email address

Go to the "Catch-all address" section and enable it, click on edit and select send to option and from the next drop down select your email address which you have verified. Click Save.

Now, you are set to use any email address from this domain. Like if you receive an email on hashnode@domainname.tk, devto@domainname.tk, or medium@domainname.tk. These all will land in your primary email address which you verified in cloudflare.

Now, while signing up on any website, you can add any email address on that website and the email will land on your webmail.

That's a wrap. Let me know your feedback on this. Please follow for more such tricks and tutorials.
item.author: blank_slate

item.title: 为什么我喜欢模式匹配 | Why I love pattern matching
item.description:
这篇文章也可以在我的联合WriteFreely 博客上阅读。

昨晚我在玩一些简单的算法问题。 所需的语言是好的 ol' JavaScript，但作为最近转换为 Elixir 和函数式编程领域的人，我继续用两种语言编写解决方案。

问题是编写一个函数，给定一个数组，返回一个包含累积和的数组。

相当标准的编码评估问题。 看似简单，但不是太难，如果你事先不知道它就无法解决。 也有很多解决方案。 请参阅此Stack Overflow 问题以获取灵感。

咖喱🍛

现在，到目前为止，您可以做的最酷的方法是使用带有 Currying 的本机map功能。

 function sumArrayCurry ( arr ) { return arr . map ( ( ( sum ) => ( value ) => ( sum += value ) )( 0 ) ) }

这恰好是 Stack Overview 上投票最多的解决方案，但是，我并不是真正的粉丝。 老实说，很难阅读。 如果我在实际的代码库中遇到这个函数，我将不得不浪费时间试图弄清楚它到底在做什么。 如果您对 Curring 的实际含义没有深入了解，那就更糟了。 这是一个 Stack Overflow 解释的链接，因为 Wikipedia 如此密集。

Array.prototype.reduce

当我第一次阅读这个问题时想到的方法是使用<some array>.reduce 。 通过阅读问题，我知道我将不得不对数组的每个元素做一些事情，然后返回一个包含结果值的新数组。

这听起来对map来说是完美的，因为它返回一个数组，但是reduce很好，因为我们可以轻松地将累积和传递给回调函数的下一次迭代。 这并不意味着你不能使用地图，只是我的思维过程是如何运作的。

 function sumArrayReduce ( arr ) { const sums = [] arr . reduce (( prev , cur , index ) => { return ( sums [ index ] = prev + cur ) }, 0 ) return sums }

我喜欢这个，因为它很容易遵循程序员的逻辑和程序的流程，如果你不明白程序在做什么，你可以很容易地查一下reduce是做什么的。 这个解决方案的一件事是它依赖于原生 JavaScript 函数。 在任何类型的编码面试中（老实说，这是唯一会出现这种情况的情况），您可能会被要求不要使用本机 API。

递归

正如我之前提到的，我是最近的 Elixir 皈依者。 由于Scheme在大学期间对我的滥用，我在多年的仇恨之后才发现对函数式编程的热爱。 由于 Elixir 解决方案可能会使用递归的东西，我想使用它而不依赖于原生 JavaScript reduce函数。

 function sumArrayRecursive ( arr ) { return sumArrayHelper ( 0 , 0 , [], arr ) } function sumArrayHelper ( prevSum , index , sums , arr ) { if ( ! arr . length ) { return sums } const curSum = arr [ index ] + prevSum sums . push ( curSum ) arr . shift () return sumArrayHelper ( curSum , index ++ , sums , arr ) }

这个解决方案确实依赖于一些原生 API，但它确实消除了reduce 。 它还遵循尾递归模式，尽管这在当前的 JavaScript 世界中意义不大（Safari 是唯一支持正确尾调用source的浏览器）。

美丽的长生不老药

模式匹配是我特别喜欢的。 对于那些不熟悉模式匹配的人来说，这意味着它听起来像：你可以根据它们的外观做事情。 当涉及到诸如案例、条件语句或在我们这里的例子中的函数定义之类的事情时，这很常见。

 defmodule ListHelper do def cumlative_sum ( list ) do p_cumlative_sum ( 0 , [], list ) end # 1 defp p_cumlative_sum ( _prev_sum , sums , []), do : Enum . reverse ( sums ) # 2 defp p_cumlative_sum ( prev_sum , sums , [ head | tail ]) do p_cumlative_sum ( prev_sum + head , [ prev_sum + head | sums ], tail ) end end

在这里，我创建了一个名为ListHelper的模块，这样我就可以在iex （交互式 Elixir）中运行程序。 我定义了一个公共函数cumlative_sum/1它将获取一个列表（Elixir 没有传统的“数组”只有链表）。 我还定义了两个私有函数来处理递归p_cumlative_sum/3 。 这些私有函数具有相同的名称和相同数量的参数，但不同的是它们匹配的模式。

第三个参数被定义为一个列表。 这种行为与 JavaScript 递归解决方案相同，我们在继续执行任何逻辑if(!arr.length) {...}之前检查列表的长度。

对我来说，这在我看来更有意义，在实际情况下，它有助于构建更清洁的解决方案。

副作用

另外，附带说明一下，Elixir 中的数据是不可变的。 这意味着没有副作用。 上述突出问题的递归 JavaScript 解决方案； 调用函数后，传递给函数的数组将被移位。

在从 JavaScript 到 Elixir 再回来时，副作用一直是我最大的抱怨。 我想以一种函数式的方式编写，但是 JavaScript 中的不一致以及所有突然出现的副作用让它变得如此困难。

概括

我不太确定这应该是什么意思，但我在解决一个简单算法的同时玩弄这两种语言很开心。 在 JavaScript 或 Elixir 方面，我绝不是专家，而且我没有花太多时间优化我的解决方案，所以在我的代码中添加一些 🧂 和 🌶。

随意留下您自己的问题解决方案，甚至改进我的解决方案。 我确信有一种方法可以在 JavaScript 中使用递归，而无需使用Array.prototype.shift ，甚至可以在 Elixir 解决方案中删除Enum.reverse/1 。

谢谢阅读！ 👨‍💻 👨‍💻



原文:

This post can also be read over on my federated WriteFreely blog.

Last night I was playing around with some simple algorithm questions. The required language was good ol' JavaScript, but being a recent convert to Elixir and the functional programming realm, I went ahead and wrote solutions in both languages.

The question was to write a function that given an array, returns an array that contains cumulative sums.

i.e.:

cumlativeSums([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]) -> [1, 3, 6, 10, 15]

Pretty standard coding assessment question. Deceptively simple, but not too hard that you can't solve it if you don't know it beforehand. There are also so many solutions out there for it. See this Stack Overflow question for inspiration.

JavaScript

Curry 🍛

Now, by far the coolest method you can do is use the native map function with Currying.

function sumArrayCurry(arr) { return arr.map( ( (sum) => (value) => (sum += value) )(0) )
}

This happens to be the top-voted solution on Stack Overview, however, I'm not really a fan. It's honestly hard to read. If I came across this function in an actual codebase I would have to waste time trying to figure out what the hell it was doing. It's even worse if you don't have a strong grasp of what Curring actually is. Here's a link to a Stack Overflow explanation since Wikipedia is so dense.

Array.prototype.reduce

The method that came to mind when I first read the question was to use <some array>.reduce. From reading the question I know that I was going to have to do something to every element of the array and then return a new array containing the resultant values.

This sounds like it would be perfect for map since it returns an array, but reduce is nice since we can easily pass the cumulative sum to the next iteration of the call-back function. This doesn't mean that you can't use a map, just how my thought process worked.

function sumArrayReduce(arr) { const sums = [] arr.reduce((prev, cur, index) => { return (sums[index] = prev + cur) }, 0) return sums
}

I like this because it's easy to follow the programmer's logic and the flow of the program, and if you don't understand what the program is doing, you can easily look up what reduce does. The one thing about this solution is that it relies on native JavaScript functions. During any sort of coding interview (which, let's be honest, is the only situation where this will come up) you'll probably be asked not to use the native API.

Recursion

As I mentioned before, I'm a recent Elixir convert. I just discovered a love of functional programming after years of hate due to the abuse that Scheme left on me during university. Being that an Elixir solution would probably use something with recursion, I wanted to use that without depending on the native JavaScript reduce function.

function sumArrayRecursive(arr) { return sumArrayHelper(0, 0, [], arr)
} function sumArrayHelper(prevSum, index, sums, arr) { if (!arr.length) { return sums } const curSum = arr[index] + prevSum sums.push(curSum) arr.shift() return sumArrayHelper(curSum, index++, sums, arr)
}

This solution does rely on some of the native API, but it does eliminate the reduce. It also follows a tail-recursive pattern, even though that doesn't mean much in the current JavaScript world (Safari is the only browser that supports proper tail calls source).

Beautiful Elixir

Elixir makes functional programming make sense and enjoyable with things like pattern matching and tail recursion. Pattern matching is what I particularly like. For those who are not familiar with pattern matching, it means what it sounds like: you can do things based on how they look. This is quite common when it comes to things like cases, conditional statements, or in our case here, function definitions.

defmodule ListHelper do def cumlative_sum(list) do p_cumlative_sum(0, [], list) end # 1 defp p_cumlative_sum(_prev_sum, sums, []), do: Enum.reverse(sums) # 2 defp p_cumlative_sum(prev_sum, sums, [head | tail]) do p_cumlative_sum(prev_sum + head, [prev_sum + head | sums], tail) end
end

Here I create a module called ListHelper just so I can run the program inside of iex (interactive Elixir). I define one public function cumlative_sum/1 which will take a list (Elixir does not have traditional "arrays" only linked lists). I also define two private functions to handle the recursion p_cumlative_sum/3. These private functions have the same name and the same number of parameters, but what's different is the pattern that they match on.

The third parameter is defined to be a list. #1 p_cumlative_sum/3 will match only when that third argument is an empty list, whereas #2 will match only when the list is not empty. This behavior is the same as the JavaScript recursive solution where we check the list's length before we proceed to do any logic if(!arr.length) {...}.

To me, this just makes a lot more sense in my mind, and in practical situations, it helps build cleaner solutions.

Side effects

Also, on a side note, data in Elixir is immutable. This means no side effects. The recursive JavaScript solution above glaring issue; the arr.shift() call, the fact that arr would be a reference to whatever array you pass, create a massive side effect. The array passed to the function will be shifted after the function is called.

Side effects have been my biggest gripe while Going from JavaScript to Elixir and back again. I want to write in a functional way, but inconsistencies in JavaScript, and all the side effects that pop off just make it so hard.

Summary

I'm not really sure what the point of this was supposed to be, but I had fun playing around with both languages while solving a simple algorithm. I am by no means an expert when it comes to JavaScript or Elixir, and I didn't spend too much time optimizing my solutions so take my code with some 🧂 and 🌶.

Feel free to leave your own solutions to the question, or even improve upon mine. I'm sure there's a way to use recursion in JavaScript without having to use Array.prototype.shift, or even a way to remove the Enum.reverse/1 in the Elixir solution.

Thanks for reading! 👨‍💻

item.title: Python 中的用户输入和 While 循环 | User Input and While Loops in Python
item.description:
大多数程序都是为了解决最终用户的问题而编写的。 为此，您通常需要从用户那里获取一些信息。 举个简单的例子，假设有人想知道他们是否已经到了可以投票的年龄。 如果你编写一个程序来回答这个问题，你需要知道用户的年龄才能提供答案。 该程序将需要要求用户输入或输入他们的年龄； 一旦程序有这个输入，它可以将它与投票年龄进行比较，以确定用户是否足够大，然后报告结果。

一旦 Python 接收到用户的输入，它就会将该输入分配给一个变量，以方便您使用。 例如，以下程序要求用户输入一些文本，然后将该消息显示给用户：

 message = input ( "Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: " ) print ( message )
Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Hi Hi

在这个例子中，当 Python 运行第一行时，用户看到提示Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: . 程序在用户输入响应时等待，并在用户按下 Enter 后继续。 响应被分配给变量message ，然后print(message)将输入显示给用户。

写清楚的提示

每次使用input()函数时，都应该包含一个清晰、易于理解的提示，告诉用户您正在寻找什么样的信息。 任何告诉用户输入内容的语句都应该有效。

 name = input ( "Please enter your name: " ) print ( f "Hello, { name } !" )
Please enter your name: Ahmed Hello, Ahmed!

在提示的末尾添加一个空格，将提示与用户的响应分开，并让用户清楚地知道在哪里输入他们的文本。

有时你会想要写一个超过一行的提示。 例如，您可能想告诉用户您为什么要求某些输入。 您可以将提示分配给一个变量并将该变量传递给input()函数。 这允许您在多行中构建提示，然后编写干净的input()语句。

 prompt = "If you tell us who you are, we can personalize the messages you see." prompt += " \n What is your first name? " name = input ( prompt ) print ( f "Hello, { name } !" )
If you tell us who you are, we can personalize the messages you see. What is your first name? Ahmed Hello, Ahmed!

使用 int() 接受数字输入

当您使用input()函数时，Python 将用户输入的所有内容都解释为字符串。 考虑以下解释器会话，它询问用户的年龄：

 >>> age = input ( "How old are you? " )
How old are you ? 27
>>> age
'27'

用户输入数字 27，但是当我们向 Python 询问 age 的值时，它返回 '27'，即输入数值的字符串表示形式。 我们知道 Python 将输入解释为字符串，因为该数字现在包含在引号中。 如果您只想打印输入，这很好用。 但是，如果您尝试将输入用作数字，则会出现错误。

 >>> age = input ( "How old are you? " )
How old are you ? 27
>>> age < = 20
Traceback ( most recent call last ): File "<stdin>" , line 1 , in < module >
TypeError: '<=' not supported between instances of 'str' and 'int'

当您尝试使用输入进行数值比较时，Python 会产生错误，因为它无法将字符串与整数进行比较：分配给年龄的字符串 '27' 无法与数值 20 进行比较。我们可以通过使用int()函数来解决这个问题，该函数告诉 Python 将输入视为数值。 The int() function converts a string representation of a number to a numerical representation. int()函数将数字的字符串表示形式转换为数字表示形式。

 >>> age = input ( "How old are you? " )
How old are you ? 27
>>> age = int ( age )
>>> age < = 20
False

当您使用数值输入进行计算和比较时，请务必先将输入值转换为数值表示。

模运算符

处理数字信息的一个有用工具是模运算符 (%) ，它将一个数字除以另一个数字并返回余数。

 >>> 4 % 3 1
>>> 2 % 4
2
>>> 3 % 3
0
>>> 7 % 2
1

模运算符不会告诉您一个数字适合另一个数字的次数。 它只是告诉你剩下的是什么。

当一个数可以被另一个数整除时，余数为 0，因此模运算符总是返回 0。您可以使用这个事实来确定一个数是偶数还是奇数：

 number = input ( "Enter a number, and I'll tell you if it's even or odd: " ) number = int ( number ) if number % 2 == 0 : print ( f " \n The number { number } is even." ) else : print ( f " \n The number { number } is odd." )
Enter a number, and I'll tell you if it's even or odd: 8 The number 8 is even.

引入while循环

相反，只要某个条件为真，或者当某个条件为真， while循环就会运行。

运行中的 while 循环

您可以使用while循环对一系列数字进行计数。 例如，以下 while 循环从 1 计数到 5：

 current_number = 1 while current_number <= 5 : print ( current_number ) current_number += 1

您每天使用的程序很可能包含while循环。 例如，一个游戏需要一个while循环来保持运行，只要你想继续玩，所以只要你要求它退出，它就可以停止运行。 如果程序在我们告诉它们停止运行之前停止运行，或者在我们想要退出之后继续运行，那么程序使用起来就不会很有趣，所以 while 循环非常有用。

让用户选择何时退出

通过将大部分代码放在一个while循环中，我们可以让程序运行到用户想要的任何时间。 我们将定义一个退出值，然后只要用户没有输入退出值，程序就会一直运行。

 prompt = " \n Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:" prompt += " \n Enter 'quit' to end the program. " message = "" while message != 'quit' : message = input ( prompt ) if message != 'quit' : print ( message )
Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hi Hi Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hello Hello Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. Ok Ok Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. quit

使用标志

在前面的示例中，我们让程序在给定条件为真时执行某些任务。 但是对于更复杂的程序，其中许多不同的事件可能导致程序停止运行呢？

例如，在一个游戏中，几个不同的事件可以结束游戏。 当玩家的船用完，他们的时间用完，或者他们应该保护的城市都被摧毁时，游戏应该结束。 如果发生这些事件中的任何一个，它就需要结束。 如果可能发生许多可能的事件来停止程序，尝试在一个while语句中测试所有这些条件变得复杂和困难。

对于只在多个条件为真时才应运行的程序，您可以定义一个变量来确定整个程序是否处于活动状态。 这个变量称为flag ，用作程序的信号。 我们可以编写我们的程序，以便它们在标志设置为 True 时运行，并在多个事件中的任何一个将标志的值设置为 False 时停止运行。 因此，我们的整体while语句只需要检查一个条件：标志当前是否为 True。 然后，我们所有的其他测试（查看是否发生了应该将标志设置为 False 的事件）可以整齐地组织在程序的其余部分中。

 prompt = " \n Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:" prompt += " \n Enter 'quit' to end the program. " active = True while active : message = input ( prompt ) if message == 'quit' : active = False else : print ( message )
Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hi Hi Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hello Hello Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Enter 'quit' to end the program. quit

使用 break 退出循环

要立即退出 while 循环而不运行循环中的任何剩余代码，而不管任何条件测试的结果如何，请使用break语句。 您可以使用它来控制执行哪些代码行，哪些不执行，因此程序仅在您想要的时候执行您想要的代码。

例如，考虑一个询问用户他们去过的地方的程序。 我们可以在用户输入 'quit' 值后立即调用 break 来停止该程序中的 while 循环：

 prompt = " \n Please enter the name of a city you have visited:" prompt += " \n (Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) " while True : city = input ( prompt ) if city == 'quit' : break else : print ( f "I'd love to go to { city . title () } !" )
Please enter the name of a city you have visited: (Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) Cairo I'd love to go to Cairo! Please enter the name of a city you have visited: (Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) New York I'd love to go to New York! Please enter the name of a city you have visited: (Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) quit

以while True开头的循环将永远运行，除非它到达一个break语句。 该程序中的循环继续要求用户输入他们去过的城市的名称，直到他们输入“退出”。 当他们输入 'quit' 时， break语句运行，导致 Python 退出循环。

您可以在任何 Python 循环中使用 break 语句。 例如，您可以使用 break 来退出正在处理列表或字典的 for 循环。

在循环中使用 continue

您可以使用continue语句根据条件测试的结果返回到循环的开头，而不是完全退出循环而不执行其其余代码。 例如，考虑一个从 1 计数到 10 但仅打印该范围内的奇数的循环：

 current_number = 0 while current_number < 10 : current_number += 1 if current_number % 2 == 0 : continue print ( current_number )

避免无限循环

每个while循环都需要一种停止运行的方法，这样它就不会永远继续运行。 例如，这个计数循环应该从 1 计数到 5：

 x = 1 while x <= 5 : print ( x ) x += 1

但如果你不小心省略了x += 1行，循环将永远运行：

 # This loop runs forever! x = 1 while x <= 5 : print ( x )

现在x的值将从 1 开始，但永远不会改变。 结果，条件测试x <= 5将始终评估为 True，而 while 循环将永远运行，打印一系列 1。

每个程序员都会不时地编写一个无限的 while 循环，尤其是当程序的循环具有微妙的退出条件时。 如果您的程序陷入无限循环，请按 CTRL-C 或关闭显示程序输出的终端窗口。

为避免编写无限循环，请测试每个while循环并确保循环在您期望的时候停止。 如果您希望程序在用户输入某个输入值时结束，请运行程序并输入该值。 如果程序没有结束，请仔细检查程序处理应该导致循环退出的值的方式。 确保程序的至少一部分可以使循环的条件为 False 或使其到达break语句。

使用带有列表和字典的 while 循环

到目前为止，我们一次只处理一条用户信息。 我们收到用户的输入，然后打印输入或对它的响应。 下一次通过 while 循环，我们将接收另一个输入值并对其做出响应。 但是为了跟踪许多用户和信息，我们需要在 while 循环中使用列表和字典。

要在处理列表时修改列表，请使用while循环。 将 while 循环与列表和字典一起使用可以让您收集、存储和组织大量输入以供以后检查和报告。

将项目从一个列表移动到另一个列表

考虑一个新注册但未经验证的网站用户列表。 在我们验证了这些用户之后，我们如何将他们移到单独的已确认用户列表中？ 一种方法是在我们验证用户时使用 while 循环将用户从未确认用户列表中拉出，然后将它们添加到单独的已确认用户列表中。 以下是该代码的样子：

 # Start with users that need to be verified, # and an empty list to hold confirmed users. unconfirmed_users = [ 'mohammed' , 'ali' , 'islam' ] confirmed_users = [] # Verify each user until there are no more unconfirmed users. # Move each verified user into the list of confirmed users. while unconfirmed_users : current_user = unconfirmed_users . pop () print ( f "Verifying user: { current_user . title () } " ) confirmed_users . append ( current_user ) # Display all confirmed users. print ( " \n The following users have been confirmed:" ) for confirmed_user in confirmed_users : print ( confirmed_user . title ())
Verifying user: Islam Verifying user: Ali Verifying user: Mohammed The following users have been confirmed: Islam Ali Mohammed

我们通过打印验证消息模拟确认每个用户，然后将其添加到已确认用户列表中。 随着未确认用户列表的缩小，已确认用户列表增加。 当未确认用户列表为空时，循环停止并打印已确认用户列表。

从列表中删除特定值的所有实例

以前我们使用remove()从列表中删除特定值。 但是，如果您想从列表中删除某个值的所有实例怎么办？

假设您有一个宠物列表，其值“猫”重复了几次。 要删除该值的所有实例，您可以运行一个while循环，直到 'cat' 不再在列表中：

 pets = [ 'dog' , 'cat' , 'dog' , 'goldfish' , 'cat' , 'rabbit' , 'cat' ] print ( pets ) while 'cat' in pets : pets . remove ( 'cat' ) print ( pets )
['dog', 'cat', 'dog', 'goldfish', 'cat', 'rabbit', 'cat'] ['dog', 'dog', 'goldfish', 'rabbit']

用用户输入填充字典

您可以在每次通过while循环时提示输入尽可能多的输入。 让我们编写一个轮询程序，其中每次通过循环都会提示参与者的姓名和响应。 我们将收集到的数据存储在字典中，因为我们希望将每个响应与特定用户联系起来：

 responses = {} # Set a flag to indicate that polling is active. polling_active = True while polling_active : # Prompt for the person's name and response. name = input ( " \n What is your name? " ) response = input ( "Which programming language would you like to learn someday? " ) # Store the response in the dictionary. responses [ name ] = response # Find out if anyone else is going to take the poll. repeat = input ( "Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) " ) if repeat == 'no' : polling_active = False # Polling is complete. Show the results. print ( " \n --- Poll Results ---" ) for name , response in responses . items (): print ( f " { name } would like to learn { response } ." )
What is your name? Ahmed Which programming language would you like to learn someday? Rust Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) yes What is your name? Mohammed Which programming language would you like to learn someday? Go Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) yes What is your name? Omar Which programming language would you like to learn someday? Python Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) no --- Poll Results --- Ahmed would like to learn Rust. Mohammed would like to learn Go. Omar would like to learn Python.


原文:

Most programs are written to solve an end user’s problem. To do so, you usually need to get some information from the user. For a simple example, let’s say someone wants to find out whether they’re old enough to vote. If you write a program to answer this question, you need to know the user’s age before you can provide an answer. The program will need to ask the user to enter, or input, their age; once the program has this input, it can compare it to the voting age to determine if the user is old enough and then report the result.

How the input() Function Works

The input() function pauses your program and waits for the user to enter some text. Once Python receives the user’s input, it assigns that input to a variable to make it convenient for you to work with. For example, the following program asks the user to enter some text, then displays that message back to the user:

message = input("Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: ")
print(message)

Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you: Hi
Hi

The input() function takes one argument: the prompt, or instructions, that we want to display to the user so they know what to do. In this example, when Python runs the first line, the user sees the prompt Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:. The program waits while the user enters their response and continues after the user presses Enter. The response is assigned to the variable message, then print(message) displays the input back to the user.

Writing Clear Prompts

Each time you use the input() function, you should include a clear, easy-to-follow prompt that tells the user exactly what kind of information you’re looking for. Any statement that tells the user what to enter should work.

name = input("Please enter your name: ")
print(f"Hello, {name}!")

Please enter your name: Ahmed
Hello, Ahmed!

Add a space at the end of your prompts to separate the prompt from the user’s response and to make it clear to your user where to enter their text.

Sometimes you’ll want to write a prompt that’s longer than one line. For example, you might want to tell the user why you’re asking for certain input. You can assign your prompt to a variable and pass that variable to the input() function. This allows you to build your prompt over several lines, then write a clean input() statement.

prompt = "If you tell us who you are, we can personalize the messages you see."
prompt += "\nWhat is your first name? " name = input(prompt)
print(f"Hello, {name}!")

If you tell us who you are, we can personalize the messages you see.
What is your first name? Ahmed
Hello, Ahmed!

Using int() to Accept Numerical Input

When you use the input() function, Python interprets everything the user enters as a string. Consider the following interpreter session, which asks for the user’s age:

>>> age = input("How old are you? ")
How old are you? 27
>>> age
'27'

The user enters the number 27, but when we ask Python for the value of age, it returns '27', the string representation of the numerical value entered. We know Python interpreted the input as a string because the number is now enclosed in quotes. If all you want to do is print the input, this works well. But if you try to use the input as a number, you’ll get an error.

>>> age = input("How old are you? ")
How old are you? 27
>>> age <= 20
Traceback (most recent call last): File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: '<=' not supported between instances of 'str' and 'int'

When you try to use the input to do a numerical comparison, Python produces an error because it can’t compare a string to an integer: the string '27' that’s assigned to age can’t be compared to the numerical value 20. We can resolve this issue by using the int() function, which tells Python to treat the input as a numerical value. The int() function converts a string representation of a number to a numerical representation.

>>> age = input("How old are you? ")
How old are you? 27
>>> age = int(age)
>>> age <= 20
False

When you use numerical input to do calculations and comparisons, be sure to convert the input value to a numerical representation first.

The Modulo Operator

A useful tool for working with numerical information is the modulo operator (%), which divides one number by another number and returns the remainder.

>>> 4 % 3 1
>>> 2 % 4
2
>>> 3 % 3
0
>>> 7 % 2
1

The modulo operator doesn’t tell you how many times one number fits into another; it just tells you what the remainder is.

When one number is divisible by another number, the remainder is 0, so the modulo operator always returns 0. You can use this fact to determine if a number is even or odd:

number = input("Enter a number, and I'll tell you if it's even or odd: ")
number = int(number) if number % 2 == 0: print(f"\nThe number {number} is even.")
else: print(f"\nThe number {number} is odd.")

Enter a number, and I'll tell you if it's even or odd: 8

The number 8 is even.

Introducing while Loops

The for loop takes a collection of items and executes a block of code once for each item in the collection. In contrast, the while loop runs as long as, or while, a certain condition is true.

The while Loop in Action

You can use a while loop to count up through a series of numbers. For example, the following while loop counts from 1 to 5:

current_number = 1 while current_number <= 5: print(current_number) current_number += 1

The programs you use every day most likely contain while loops. For example, a game needs a while loop to keep running as long as you want to keep playing, and so it can stop running as soon as you ask it to quit. Programs wouldn’t be fun to use if they stopped running before we told them to or kept running even after we wanted to quit, so while loops are quite useful.

Letting the User Choose When to Quit

We can make program runs as long as the user wants by putting most of the code inside a while loop. We’ll define a quit value and then keep the program running as long as the user has not entered the quit value.

prompt = "\nTell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:"
prompt += "\nEnter 'quit' to end the program. " message = ""
while message != 'quit': message = input(prompt) if message != 'quit': print(message)


Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hi
Hi

Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hello
Hello

Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. Ok
Ok

Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. quit

Using a Flag

In the previous example, we had the program perform certain tasks while a given condition was true. But what about more complicated programs in which many different events could cause the program to stop running?

For example, in a game, several different events can end the game. When the player runs out of ships, their time runs out, or the cities they were supposed to protect are all destroyed, the game should end. It needs to end if any one of these events happens. If many possible events might occur to stop the program, trying to test all these conditions in one while statement becomes complicated and difficult.

For a program that should run only as long as many conditions are true, you can define one variable that determines whether or not the entire program is active. This variable, called a flag, acts as a signal to the program. We can write our programs so they run while the flag is set to True and stop running when any of several events sets the value of the flag to False. As a result, our overall while statement needs to check only one condition: whether or not the flag is currently True. Then, all our other tests (to see if an event has occurred that should set the flag to False) can be neatly organized in the rest of the program.

prompt = "\nTell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:"
prompt += "\nEnter 'quit' to end the program. " active = True
while active: message = input(prompt) if message == 'quit': active = False else: print(message)


Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hi
Hi

Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. Hello
Hello

Tell me something, and I will repeat it back to you:
Enter 'quit' to end the program. quit

Using break to Exit a Loop

To exit a while loop immediately without running any remaining code in the loop, regardless of the results of any conditional test, use the break statement. The break statement directs the flow of your program; you can use it to control which lines of code are executed and which aren’t, so the program only executes code that you want it to, when you want it to.

For example, consider a program that asks the user about places they’ve visited. We can stop the while loop in this program by calling break as soon as the user enters the 'quit' value:

prompt = "\nPlease enter the name of a city you have visited:"
prompt += "\n(Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) " while True: city = input(prompt) if city == 'quit': break else: print(f"I'd love to go to {city.title()}!")


Please enter the name of a city you have visited:
(Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) Cairo
I'd love to go to Cairo!

Please enter the name of a city you have visited:
(Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) New York 
I'd love to go to New York!

Please enter the name of a city you have visited:
(Enter 'quit' when you are finished.) quit

A loop that starts with while True will run forever unless it reaches a break statement. The loop in this program continues asking the user to enter the names of cities they’ve been to until they enter 'quit'. When they enter 'quit', the break statement runs, causing Python to exit the loop.

You can use the break statement in any of Python’s loops. For example, you could use break to quit a for loop that’s working through a list or a dictionary.

Using continue in a Loop

Rather than breaking out of a loop entirely without executing the rest of its code, you can use the continue statement to return to the beginning of the loop based on the result of a conditional test. For example, consider a loop that counts from 1 to 10 but prints only the odd numbers in that range:

current_number = 0 while current_number < 10: current_number += 1 if current_number % 2 == 0: continue print(current_number)

the continue statement tells Python to ignore the rest of the loop and return to the beginning.

Avoiding Infinite Loops

Every while loop needs a way to stop running so it won’t continue to run forever. For example, this counting loop should count from 1 to 5:

x = 1
while x <= 5: print(x) x += 1

But if you accidentally omit the line x += 1, the loop will run forever:

# This loop runs forever!
x = 1
while x <= 5: print(x)

Now the value of x will start at 1 but never change. As a result, the conditional test x <= 5 will always evaluate to True and the while loop will run forever, printing a series of 1s.

Every programmer accidentally writes an infinite while loop from time to time, especially when a program’s loops have subtle exit conditions. If your program gets stuck in an infinite loop, press CTRL-C or just close the terminal window displaying your program’s output.

To avoid writing infinite loops, test every while loop and make sure the loop stops when you expect it to. If you want your program to end when the user enters a certain input value, run the program and enter that value. If the program doesn’t end, scrutinize the way your program handles the value that should cause the loop to exit. Make sure at least one part of the program can make the loop’s condition False or cause it to reach a break statement.

Using a while Loop with Lists and Dictionaries

So far, we’ve worked with only one piece of user information at a time. We received the user’s input and then printed the input or a response to it. The next time through the while loop, we’d receive another input value and respond to that. But to keep track of many users and pieces of information, we’ll need to use lists and dictionaries with our while loops.

A for loop is effective for looping through a list, but you shouldn’t modify a list inside a for loop because Python will have trouble keeping track of the items in the list. To modify a list as you work through it, use a while loop. Using while loops with lists and dictionaries allows you to collect, store, and organize lots of input to examine and report on later.

Moving Items from One List to Another

Consider a list of newly registered but unverified users of a website. After we verify these users, how can we move them to a separate list of confirmed users? One way would be to use a while loop to pull users from the list of unconfirmed users as we verify them and then add them to a separate list of confirmed users. Here’s what that code might look like:

# Start with users that need to be verified,
# and an empty list to hold confirmed users.
unconfirmed_users = ['mohammed', 'ali', 'islam']
confirmed_users = [] # Verify each user until there are no more unconfirmed users.
# Move each verified user into the list of confirmed users.
while unconfirmed_users: current_user = unconfirmed_users.pop() print(f"Verifying user: {current_user.title()}") confirmed_users.append(current_user) # Display all confirmed users.
print("\nThe following users have been confirmed:")
for confirmed_user in confirmed_users: print(confirmed_user.title())

Verifying user: Islam
Verifying user: Ali
Verifying user: Mohammed

The following users have been confirmed:
Islam
Ali
Mohammed

We simulate confirming each user by printing a verification message and then adding them to the list of confirmed users. As the list of unconfirmed users shrinks, the list of confirmed users grows. When the list of unconfirmed users is empty, the loop stops and the list of confirmed users is printed.

Removing All Instances of Specific Values from a List

Previously we used remove() to remove a specific value from a list. The remove() function worked because the value we were interested in appeared only once in the list. But what if you want to remove all instances of a value from a list?

Say you have a list of pets with the value 'cat' repeated several times. To remove all instances of that value, you can run a while loop until 'cat' is no longer in the list:

pets = ['dog', 'cat', 'dog', 'goldfish', 'cat', 'rabbit', 'cat']
print(pets) while 'cat' in pets: pets.remove('cat') print(pets)

['dog', 'cat', 'dog', 'goldfish', 'cat', 'rabbit', 'cat']
['dog', 'dog', 'goldfish', 'rabbit']

Filling a Dictionary with User Input

You can prompt for as much input as you need in each pass through a while loop. Let’s make a polling program in which each pass through the loop prompts for the participant’s name and response. We’ll store the data we gather in a dictionary, because we want to connect each response with a particular user:

responses = {} # Set a flag to indicate that polling is active.
polling_active = True while polling_active: # Prompt for the person's name and response.
 name = input("\nWhat is your name? ") response = input("Which programming language would you like to learn someday? ") # Store the response in the dictionary.
 responses[name] = response # Find out if anyone else is going to take the poll.
 repeat = input("Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) ") if repeat == 'no': polling_active = False # Polling is complete. Show the results.
print("\n--- Poll Results ---")
for name, response in responses.items(): print(f"{name} would like to learn {response}.")


What is your name? Ahmed
Which programming language would you like to learn someday? Rust
Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) yes

What is your name? Mohammed
Which programming language would you like to learn someday? Go
Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) yes

What is your name? Omar
Which programming language would you like to learn someday? Python
Would you like to let another person respond? (yes/ no) no

--- Poll Results ---
Ahmed would like to learn Rust.
Mohammed would like to learn Go.
Omar would like to learn Python.
item.author: ahmedgouda
